title: Adai
description: Enjoy the wholesome goodness of 'Adai', a traditional South Indian crepe made with a mix of lentils and rice. This recipe is crispy on the outside, soft on the inside, and packed with protein, making it perfect for enjoying anytime.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
main_ingredients: Split skinless green gram (dhuli moong dal)

ingredients.raw: Â½ cup split pigeon peas (toovar dal), soaked for 1 hour and drained
ingredients.name: Â½ cup split pigeon peas , soaked for 1 hour and drained

ingredients.raw: Â¼ cup split red lentils (masoor dal), soaked for 1 hour and drained
ingredients.name: Â¼ cup split red lentils , soaked for 1 hour and drained

ingredients.raw: Â½ cup split skinless green gram (dhuli moong dal), soaked for 1 hour and drained
ingredients.name: Â½ cup split skinless green gram , soaked for 1 hour and drained

ingredients.raw: Â¼ cup split Bengal gram (chana dal), soaked for 1 hour and drained
ingredients.name: Â¼ cup split Bengal gram , soaked for 1 hour and drained

ingredients.raw: Â¼ cup short grain rice, soaked for 1 hour and drained
ingredients.name: Â¼ cup short grain rice, soaked for 1 hour and drained

steps: Put split pigeon peas into a grinder jar, add split red lentils, split green gram, split Bengal gram, rice, ginger, curry leaves, dried red chillies, cumin seeds, asafoetida, salt and 1Â½ cups water and grind to fine batter.Â Transfer into a deep pan and whisk well.

Heat a non-stick tawa. Spread a ladleful of batter into a round not-too-thin disc and drizzle some oil around. Cook on medium heat for 2-3 minutes. Flip and cook for 2-3 minutes more. Make more adais similarly with the remaining batter.
Transfer the adais onto individual serving plates and serve hot with coconut chutney.
tags: Split skinless green gram (dhuli moong dal)

title: Afghani Anda Curry
description: Egg curries can be made in a variety of ways and here it is cooked in Afghani style. Full of flavour, this curry will leave a lasting impression on you and make you want to have it often.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: Heat 2 tablespoons ghee in a pan, add onions, garlic, ginger, cashewnuts, green chillies and coriander stems, mix and sautÃ© till onions are translucent.
Transfer the mixture onto a plate and allow to cool completely.
Transfer the mixture into a grinder jar, add mint leaves, coriander leaves and yogurt and grind to fine paste.
Heat 2 tablespoons ghee in another pan, add cinnamon and black peppercorns, mix and sautÃ© till fragrant.
Add ground paste, coriander powder and Â¼ teaspoon turmeric powder and cook on medium heat for 8-10 minutes.
Meanwhile score the eggs. Heat 2 tablespoons ghee in a shallow non-stick pan, add eggs, remaining turmeric powder and red chilli powder and sautÃ© till golden brown. Drain and set aside.
Add salt and 1Â½ cups water to the gravy, mix well and cook for 2 minutes.
Pour the gravy into a serving plate/ bowl. Arrange eggs on top and serve hot.

title: Air Fryer Crumbed Chicken Schnitzel
description: All chicken lovers will simply love this delicacy where chicken thighs are first pounded, then rolled in flour, egg and breadcrumbs and fried to a golden perfection. The name comes from German word â€˜snizâ€™ which means slice.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: Place the chicken pieces on a chopping board lined with a plastic sheet. Place another plastic sheet over the chicken and beat with a steak hammer. Transfer the chicken into a bowl.
Add garlic paste, salt and crushed black peppercorns and rub well all over the chicken. Set aside to marinate for 30 minutes.
Spread refined flour on a plate, add salt and mix well.
Spread panko breadcrumbs on another plate. Add salt, crushed black peppercorns, dried mixed herbs and red chilli flakes and mix well.
Break eggs into a bowl. Add salt and crushed black peppercorns and whisk well with a fork.
Preheat the air fryer at 180Â°C for 4 minutes.
Roll the chicken pieces in refined flour mixture, dip in egg mixture and roll in panko breadcrumb mixture.
Spray some oil in the air fryer basket, arrange the chicken pieces in it, fit the basket to the air fryer and air fry for 12 minutes.
Bring the basket out, remove the chicken and place them on a serving plate. Serve hot with coleslaw.

title: Anda Bhurji
description: Anda bhurji is the Indian version of scrambled eggs. Eggs cooked with onions, green chillies and some masala powders, the end result is simply delicious. A popular street food in Mumbai, served with pavs, it is enjoyed by all who love eggs.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: 2-3 tablespoons chopped fresh coriander leaves + to sprinkle
ingredients.name: -3 tablespoons chopped fresh coriander leaves + to sprinkle

steps: Chop onion and green chillies finely.
Heat oil and butter in a pan, add cumin seeds and once they start to change colour, add green chillies and onions, mix and sautÃ© till translucent. Add tomatoes, mix and cook for 3-4 minutes
Add salt, red chilli powder, turmeric powder, garam masala powder and coriander powder, mix well and saute for 1-2 minutes.
Break eggs in the pan and cook, stirring continuously, for 3-4 minutes.
Add coriander leaves and mix well.
Transfer into a serving bowl, sprinkle coriander leaves on top and serve hot with pavs.

title: Avocado Omelette with Salsa
description: Avocado slices and salsa give this omelette a wonderful taste and crunch that is simply irresistible. You will want to eat it daily. This is Sanjeev Kapoor exclusive recipe.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: For each portion break 2 eggs in a bowl and beat well.

To make salsa take 8-10 dill sprigs in a bowl, add 6-8 parsley sprigs, 5-6 cherry tomatoes, 5-6 mint sprigs, 2 green chillies, crushed black peppercorns, salt and 2 teaspoons olive oil. Squeeze 1 wedge of lemon and mix well.
Peel Â¼ avocado and cut into thin slices. Drizzle some lemon juice on top and set aside.
Heat 2 teaspoons butter in a small non-stick pan. Add beaten eggs, sprinkle salt and mix lightly once. Cook on medium heat for 2-3 minutes. Fold in half and continue to cook for 2 minutes more.
Transfer onto a serving plate and place avocado slices on top, place the salsa on top of avocado and serve with toasted bread slices.

title: Besan Ke Gune
description: This recipe will freshen memories of the soil of Rajasthan and its royal flavour. This snack is known for its unique shape and delicious flavour. Though the ingredients are very simple, but when made in the correct way it becomes special.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
main_ingredients: Split skinless black gram (dhuli urad dal)

ingredients.raw: Â½ cup-soaked split skinless black gram (dhuli urad dal)
ingredients.name: Â½ cup-soaked split skinless black gram

ingredients.raw: 2 tablespoons oil + to grease +to deep fry
ingredients.name: oil + to grease +to deep fry

steps: Put the soaked dal into a grinder jar, add â…“ cup water and grind to a fine mixture.
Take gram flour in a bowl, add cumin seeds, asafoetida, crushed black peppercorns, red chilli powder, salt, carom seeds, baking soda and dried fenugreek leaves and mix well.
Add ground paste and mix well. Add 2 tablespoons oil and knead to a soft dough. Set aside for 10-15 minutes.
Heat sufficient oil in a kadai.

Divide the dough into 3-4 equal portions and shape them into balls. Grease the worktop with oil and roll each dough ball into semi thick sheet. Trim the sides to make a rectangle and further cut into thin strips. Fold each strip around your finger to form a small cylinder.
Slide in these cylinders, a few at a time, into hot oil and deep-fry till golden brown and crisp. Drain on absorbent paper and allow to cool.
Arrange them in a serving bowl and serve or store in an airtight container to be served when required.
tags: Split skinless black gram (dhuli urad dal)

title: Challah
description: Challah, also known as berches in Central Europe, is usually braided. It is a special bread which the Jews make on ceremonial occasions such as Shabbat and major Jewish holidays.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: â…› cup olive oil + for greasing
ingredients.name: â…› cup olive oil + for greasing

steps: Take bread flour in a stand mixer jar, add castor sugar, salt and active dry yeast.
Fit the jar on stand mixer, add 1Â½ cups (325 ml) water and process to a soft dough.Â Add olive oil and process again.
Grease a bowl with some olive oil, transfer the dough into the bowl, cover with a cling film and set aside for 1.5- 2 hours.
Knock back the dough. Dust some bread flour on the worktop and transfer the dough on it.
Divide the dough into 9 equal portions and roll into balls. Roll each ball into thin sticks.

Spray some water on the sticks. Sprinkle white sesame seeds on a tray and one of the sticks and set aside.Similarly sprinkle black sesame seeds on another tray and on second stick. Coat one more stick with both white and black sesame seeds.
Place the coated sticks on worktop and pleat them. Repeat this with the remaining sticks.
Grease a baking tray with some olive oil and arrange challah breads on it. Cover with a moist muslin cloth and set aside to rise for 45 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 180Â° C.
Apply egg wash on top of each pleat, keep the tray in the preheated oven and bake for 15-20 minutes.
Bring the tray out of the oven, apply melted butter on top and allow to cool.
Slice, place on a serving plate and serve.

title: Chana Masala
description: Chana masala, another Punjabi speciality which is a favourite with most Punjabi homes and cooked often. Served with paranthe or rotis or naans, chana masala is a dish that leaves you craving for more.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: Heat a pressure cooker, add chickpeas, salt, Â½ teaspoon red chilli powder, black cardamom, green cardamoms, cloves, cinnamon, bay leaf, 1 tablespoon chole masala and 3 cups water and mix well.
Cover with the lid and cook under pressure, on medium heat, till the pressure is released 5-6 times.
Open the lid once the pressure settles completely. Strain the chickpeas, discard the whole spices and reserve the stock.
Heat ghee in a non-stick kadai, add cumin seeds and once they start to change colour, add onions and mix well. Add salt, mix and sautÃ© till onions turn a golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and green chillies, mix and sautÃ© for 2-3 minutes.

Add tomato puree and mix well. Add Â½ teaspoon red chilli powder, turmeric powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, dried mango powder and Â½ teaspoon chole masala, mix well, cover and cook till oil separates.
Add boiled chickpeas and mix well. Add 1Â½ cups chickpea stock, mix well and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Add coriander leaves and mix well. Transfer into a serving bowl, garnish with ginger strips and green chillies and serve hot with paranthe.

title: Chane Ka Khatta
description: Tangy, warming and deeply comforting, this is the kind of dish that makes you feel at home .This traditional Himachali curry is made with soft black chana simmered in a perfectly balanced gravy of spices and sourness using simple ingredients.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: Heat a pressure cooker, add brown Bengal grams, salt, 1 teaspoon dried mango powder, 1 teaspoon coriander powder, Â½ teaspoon red chilli powder and 3Â½ cups water and mix well.
Close the cooker and cook under pressure on medium heat till the pressure is released 3-4 times.
Open the lid once the pressure settles completely. Strain and keep the grams and the stock separately.

Heat mustard oil in a non-stick kadai till it begins to smoke then add asafoetida and cumin seeds and mix. Once cumin seeds start to change colour, add fennel seeds and fenugreek seeds, mix and sautÃ© for 30 seconds.

Add dried red chillies and mix well. Add turmeric powder, remaining red chilli powder, remaining coriander powder, remaining dried mango powder, rice flour, gram flour and salt, mix well and sautÃ© on low heat for 2-3 minutes.
Add boiled grams, mix well and sautÃ© for 4-5 minutes.
Add cooking stock, mix well and cook on medium heat till mixture thickens.
Add garam masala powder, mint leaves and jaggery, mix well and cook till jaggery melts.
Transfer into a serving bowl, garnish with coriander sprig and serve hot with steamed rice.

title: Chicken Keema Kulcha
description: Traditionally kulchas are cooked in a tandoor. But here we show you how to make it in an air fryer. Delicious protein packed kulchas stuffed with a spicy chicken mince mixture, these kulchas are to die for.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: To make the dough, sift refined flour, sugar and salt into a bowl.
Add instant dry yeast, 50 millilitres warm water and warm milk and knead to soft dough. Â Add ghee and knead again. Cover with a moist muslin cloth and set aside for 1 hour.

To make the stuffing, heat oil in a non-stick pan, add cumin seeds and once they start to change colour, add coriander seeds, garlic, ginger, green chillies and onion, mix and sautÃ© till onion turns golden brown.
Add red chilli powder, coriander powder and chaat masala and mix well.
Add chicken mince, garam masala powder and salt, mix well and cook on high heat for 2-3 minutes.
Add tomato ketchup and coriander leaves and mix well. Take the pan off the heat and allow to cool completely.

Divide the dough and the stuffing into equal parts, roll out the dough into thick discs and place a portion of the chicken mixture on each disc. Gather the edges and seal properly. Roll out into thick kulchas.
Spray some oil on kulchas and sprinkle onion seeds on top.
Preheat the air fryer at 200Â°C for 2-3 minutes. Arrange the kulchas in air fryer basket, fit the basket to the air fryer and air fry for 6 minutes.
Bring the basket out, remove the kulchas and place them in a serving platter, brush some melted butter on top and serve.

title: Chicken Manchurian
description: A popular Indo-Chinese dish, Chicken Manchurian has deep-fried boneless chicken pieces tossed in a tangy, spicy and flavourful sauce. Invented in India, the sauce is flavoured with ginger, garlic, green chillies, soy sauce and tomato ketchup.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: 2-3 tablespoons chopped spring onion greens + for garnish
ingredients.name: -3 tablespoons chopped spring onion greens + for garnish

steps: Cut chicken into 1-inch pieces and transfer into a bowl. Add salt, crushed black peppercorns, 1Â½ teaspoons light soy sauce, cornflour and egg white and mix well.
Heat sufficient oil in a kadai. Gently slide the chicken pieces into hot oil and deep-fry till golden brown and crisp. Drain on absorbent paper and set aside.
Cut onion into 1-inch pieces and separate the layers. Deseed and cut green capsicum into 1-inch triangles.
Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a non-stick wok, add garlic, ginger and green chillies, mix and saute till golden brown.
Add red chilli paste, mix and sautÃ© for 2 minutes.
Add onion, green capsicum and mix well. Add oyster sauce, 1Â½ teaspoons light soy sauce, red chilli sauce and brown sugar and mix well.
Add chicken stock, salt and crushed black peppercorns and mix well. Once the mixture comes to a boil, add cornflour slurry, mix well and cook till the mixture thickens.
Add chicken pieces and cook till the sauce coats all the chicken pieces well.
Add spring onion greens and mix well.
Transfer into a serving bowl, sprinkle spring onion greens and serve hot.

title: Chicken Tikka
description: Juicy, smoky yogurt-marinated chicken skewers grilled to perfection and finished with butter and chaat masala for a classic, flavorful Indian appetizer.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: Â½ teaspoon dried fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi) powder
ingredients.name: Â½ teaspoon dried fenugreek leaves  powder

steps: Place a strainer over a bowl, keep a piece of muslin cloth in the strainer and add yogurt.
Gather the edges to make a potli, keep a piece of stone or any other weight on it and set aside for 1 hour so that excess water drains away.
Cut chicken into 2-inch pieces and transfer on a plate, add salt and 1Â½ teaspoons Kashmiri red chilli powder. Squeeze the juice of Â½ lemon and mix well. Set aside to marinate for 30-40 minutes.

Transfer the hung yogurt into a bowl, add ginger-garlic paste, Kashmiri red chilli paste, salt, garam masala powder, 1 teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder, dried fenugreek leaves powder and roasted gram flour.
Squeeze the juice of Â½ lemon and add mustard oil and whisk well. Add the chicken pieces and mix well. Keep the bowl in the refrigerator to marinate for 1 hour.
Place a small earthen bowl in middle of the chicken. Keep a piece of hot coal in it and drizzle some ghee over the coal. Cover and set aside for 1 minute.
Thread the chicken pieces onto wooden skewers.
Heat a non-stick grill pan and brush it with some melted butter. Place the skewers in it and drizzle some more melted butter over them and cook on medium heat for 5-6 minutes.
Flip, drizzle some more melted butter and cook for 5-6 minutes more.
Finally drizzle some more melted butter on top.
Transfer onto a serving plate, brush some melted butter and sprinkle chaat masala on top.
Serve hot with green chutney, onion slices and mint sprigs.

title: Chilli Chicken Cheese Toast
description: Though this chicken toast is perfect for children, it is savoured by adults too. It is a delicious combination of crispy toasted bread slices topped with mild and flavourful chilli chicken and a layer of melted cheese which make every bite gooey and fun.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: Â¾ cup boiled and shredded chicken
ingredients.name: Â¾ cup boiled and shredded chicken

steps: Deseed and chop green capsicum.
Heat 2 tablespoons butter in a shallow non-stick pan, add onion and sautÃ© till translucent.
Add green capsicum, mix and sautÃ© for 1 minute.
Transfer into a bowl, add cheese, chicken, green chillies and salt and mix well.
Place the bread slices on worktop and apply butter on all the slices.
Spread a generous layer of chicken stuffing on 4 slices and cover with remaining 4 slices. Apply butter on top of all the sandwiches.
Preheat a sandwich griller and place 2 sandwiches in it. Grill till golden brown and crisp. Cook the remaining 2 sandwiches similarly.
Halve each sandwich, place them on a serving platter and serve hot with tomato ketchup.

title: Chilli Garlic Mushroom Noodles
description:
Make this dish when you donâ€™t have sufficient time to make a wholesome meal. It is tasty, flavourful and nutritious too.
This is Sanjeev Kapoor exclusive recipe.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: 4-5 dried shitake mushrooms, soaked, drained and sliced
ingredients.name: -5 dried shitake mushrooms, soaked, drained and sliced

steps: Heat oil in a non-stick kadai, add garlic and sautÃ© till golden brown.
Add spring onion bulbs, mixed capsicums, shimeji mushrooms, button mushrooms, shitake mushrooms, 2 tablespoons spring onion greens, salt and fresh red chillies, mix and sautÃ© for 2-3 minutes.
Add noodles, soy sauce and vinegar, mix and cook for 3-4 minutes.
Add remaining spring onion greens and mix well.
Transfer into a serving bowl and serve hot.

title: Chilli Garlic Spaghetti
description: A simple spaghetti dish made in a jiffy without compromising the taste. Fresh red chillies, garlic and parsley add their flavour making the dish delicious. This is Sanjeev Kapoor exclusive recipe.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: 2 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil + to drizzle
ingredients.name: extra virgin olive oil + to drizzle

steps: Boil sufficient water in a deep pan. Add salt and spaghetti, mix and cook for 8-10 minutes. Drain and set aside.
Meanwhile chop fresh red chillies and parsley sprigs. Slice garlic cloves.
Heat extra virgin olive oil in a non-stick pan, add garlic and sautÃ© till light golden brown in colour.
Add fresh red chillies, mix and sautÃ© for 30 seconds. Add spaghetti and add to the pan.
Add salt and parsley, mix and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Sprinkle parmesan cheese powder and drizzle extra virgin olive oil and mix.
Transfer into a serving bowl, sprinkle some parmesan cheese powder and drizzle some extra virgin olive oil and serve hot.

title: Chimichurri Grilled Prawns
description: Spicy and tangy these prawns, marinated in chimichurri sauce and grilled to perfection, make delicious snack or starter. So if you are planning a cocktail party, make these prawns â€“ they will be much appreciated.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: 12-16 medium prawns, peeled and deveined, tails intact
ingredients.name: -16 medium prawns, peeled and deveined, tails intact

steps: Chop parsley and oregano and transfer into a bowl.
Add crushed black peppercorns, red chilli flakes, green chillies, garlic, olive oil, salt and red wine vinegar mix well.
Add onion and mix well.
Add prawns and mix well. Thread the prawns on wooden skewers.
Heat a non-stick grill pan. Add little butter and place the prepared skewers in the pan. Brush the remaining sauce and grill for 2-3 minutes on each side.
Arrange them on a serving platter and serve hot with lemon wedges.

title: Chingri Malai Curry
description: Every region has a different definition for comfort food. For Bengal it is Chingri Malai Curry. Soft prawns, fresh tender coconut and a gravy thatâ€™s simply full of flavour. This one is from the coast, done the Bengali way.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: Â½ cup tender coconut flesh strips + for garnish
ingredients.name: Â½ cup tender coconut flesh strips + for garnish

steps: Remove the eyes, tentacles and shells of prawns, devein and transfer into a bowl. Wash thoroughly and drain completely.
Add salt and Â¼ teaspoon turmeric powder and mix well. Set aside for 10 minutes.
Heat oil in a pan, add marinated prawns and sear on high heat for 2-3 minutes. Drain and set aside.
Add ghee to the same pan. Add bay leaves, onion paste and garlic paste, mix and sautÃ© on medium heat till golden brown.
Add Â¼ teaspoon turmeric powder and red chilli powder and mix well.
Add Â½ cup water, mix well and cook for 2 minutes.
Add Â½ cup coconut milk, mix well and cook for 1 minute.
Add 1Â¾ cups coconut milk, mix and cook for 4-5 minutes.
Add green chillies, salt and sugar and mix well. Add Â¾ cup water and mix well.
Once the mixture comes to a boil, add prawns, garam masala powder and tender coconut strips and mix well. Cook for 3-4 minutes.
Transfer into a serving bowl, garnish with slit green chillies, tender coconut strips and serve hot with steamed rice.

title: Chukauni
description: Chukauni is a traditional Nepali salad that originated in Palpa district of Nepal. Made with boiled potatoes and yogurt, and tempered with mustard oil and fenugreek seeds, its tangy, creamy flavour is very appetizing.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: 1 tablespoon chopped fresh coriander leaves + for garnish
ingredients.name: chopped fresh coriander leaves + for garnish

steps: Peel potatoes and cut into 1-inch pieces. Peel and slice onions thinly. Slice fresh red chillies diagonally.
Take yogurt in a bowl, add roasted cumin powder, dried mango powder, potatoes, onions, fresh red chillies, chopped coriander leaves and salt and mix well.
To make tempering, heat mustard oil in a pan till it begins to smoke. Add fenugreek seeds, turmeric powder and red chilli powder and mix well.
Add this tempering to potato mixture and mix well.
Transfer into a serving bowl, garnish with fresh red chillies and coriander leaves and serve with steamed rice.

title: Coconut Tomato Chutney
description: Tomatoes add their natural tartness to this flavourful chutney. It is not very spicy and tastes great with idli or dosa or uttappam. This is Sanjeev Kapoor exclusive recipe.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
main_ingredients: Split skinless black grams (dhuli urad dal)

ingredients.raw: 1 teaspoon split skinless black gram (dhuli urad dal)
ingredients.name: split skinless black gram

steps: Roughly chop tomatoes.
Heat oil in a pan, add ginger, garlic, curry leaves and dried red chillies, mix and sautÃ© for 1 minute.
Add tomatoes, mix and saute for 4-5 minutes on medium heat.
Add salt, sugar and Â½ cup water and mix well. Cover and cook on medium heat for 5-6 minutes more. Allow to cool completely.
Transfer the cooled mixture into a grinder jar. Add coconut and grind to fine paste. Transfer this paste into a bowl.
To make tempering, heat oil in a pan, add split skinless black gram and mustard seeds and once they start to splutter, add asafoetida and curry leaves and sautÃ© for a few seconds.
Add this to ground chutney in the bowl and mix well. Transfer into a serving bowl and serve.
tags: Split skinless black grams (dhuli urad dal)

title: Cranberry Mango Salsa
description: Did you know that dried cranberries are not only delicious but also packed with incredible health benefits? Here we bring you an exciting recipe that brings together the flavours of mango and cranberries in a delightful salsa.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: Â½ cup Pro V Regal Jumbo Cranberries
ingredients.name: Â½ cup Pro V Regal Jumbo Cranberries

steps: Chop Pro V Regal Jumbo Cranberries and transfer in a bowl.
Add mango, onion, tomato, green chillies, coriander leaves and salt and mix well.
Transfer into a serving bowl and serve with nachos.

title: Crunchy Vegetable Nuggets
description: Who doesnâ€™t love to have crispy snack? But if you want to avoid deep-fried ones then this recipe is for you. Protein rich with soya mince, these nuggets are snack you will not be irresistible.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: 1 cup boiled and crushed green peas
ingredients.name: boiled and crushed green peas

steps: Take boiled potatoes in a bowl, add soya mince, green peas, broccoli, garam masala powder, chaat masala, salt, dried breadcrumbs, green chillies and mayonnaise and mix well.
Preheat the air fryer at 200Â° C for 5 minutes.
Divide the mixture into small equal portions and shape each portion into a nugget.
Spray some oil in air fryer basket and arrange the prepared nuggets in it. Spray some oil on them, fit the basket to the air fryer and air fry for 5 minutes.
Bring the basket out, flip the nuggets, spray some more oil over them and air fry for 5 minutes more.
Bring the basket out, transfer the nuggets onto a serving plate and serve hot with choice of your dip.

title: Dahi Upma
description: Upma is one of the most popularly eaten South Indian snack made with semolina. One can add different vegetables to add a variety. Here we have used yogurt which makes the snack soft and at the same time a delicious sour taste makes it more alluring.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: 1 teaspoon split skinless black gram (dhuli urad dal)
ingredients.name: split skinless black gram

steps: Take yogurt in a bowl, add 3 cups water and whisk well.
Heat a non-stick kadai, add semolina and dry roast on medium heat till light brown. Transfer into a bowl and set aside.
Heat ghee in same kadai, add mustard seeds and once they begin to splutter, add asafoetida and split black gram, mix and saute for 30 seconds.
Add green chilli, curry leaves and ginger, mix and sautÃ© for 30 seconds.
Add onion, mix and sautÃ© on medium heat for 3-4 minutes.
Add green peas, salt and sugar, mix well and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Add dry roasted semolina and mix well. Add yogurt mixture and mix well.
Add coriander leaves, mix well, cover and cook on medium heat for 6-8 minutes.
Transfer into a round bowl and press well, demould onto a serving plate, garnish with coriander sprig and serve hot.
tags: Split skinless black gram (dhuli urad dal)

title: Dhaba Style Moong Tadka
description: A lot of dhaba style dishes leave life long memories because of their highway charm and taste. So here we bring you this rustic and bold and flavourful dish that you can cook at home.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: 2 cups cooked whole green gram (sabut moong)
ingredients.name: cooked whole green gram

ingredients.raw: Â¼ teaspoon dried fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi) powder
ingredients.name: Â¼ teaspoon dried fenugreek leaves  powder

steps: Heat oil in a non-stick kadai, add cumin seeds and once they start to change colour, add garlic and green chillies, mix and sautÃ© for 30 seconds.
Add onions, mix and sautÃ© till golden brown. Add tomato puree and salt, mix well, cover and cook till oil separates.
Add red chilli powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder and cumin powder and mix well.
Add Â½ cup water and mix well. Add cashewnut paste and mix well.
Add cooked green gram, garam masala powder, kasoori methi powder and coriander leaves and mix well.Â Add 1 cup water, mix, cover and cook for 4-5 minutes.
Transfer into a serving bowl, garnish with coriander sprig and serve hot with paranthas.

title: Easy Seekh Kebab
description: If youâ€™ve ever tasted seekh kebabs itâ€™s not just about food itâ€™s about the smoke, the aroma, and that first juicy bite. With the right balance of spices, proper technique, and controlled heat, these kebabs turn out soft, juicy, and deeply satisfying.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: Â½ teaspoon roasted and crushed cumin seeds
ingredients.name: Â½ teaspoon roasted and crushed cumin seeds

steps: Grate onion, put it on a piece of muslin cloth, bring the edges together and squeeze excess water and set aside.

Put chicken mince into a food processor jar, add ginger-garlic paste, green chillies, roasted and crushed cumin seeds, crushed black peppercorns, garam masala powder, green cardamom powder, coriander leaves, mint leaves, fried onions, salt and grated onion and process to a fine mixture.
Add butter, egg white and fresh breadcrumbs and continue to grind till well combined.
Transfer the ground mixture into a piping bag and snip off the pointed edge of the bag.
Grease a non-stick grill pan with butter and pipe long cylinders of chicken mixture in the pan. Cook on medium heat for 2-3 minutes, flip, brush some melted butter and cook for 3-4 minutes.
Transfer onto a serving platter, sprinkle chaat masala over the kebabs and serve hot with some salad and green chutney.

title: Egg Rice Paper Parcels
description: These neat parcels are as delicious as they look. Rice paper sheets stuffed with a tasty chicken mixture, folded to make parcels and shallow fried to a perfect golden hue.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: Â½ cup boiled and chopped chicken
ingredients.name: Â½ cup boiled and chopped chicken

steps: Break eggs into a bowl, add salt and crushed black peppercorns and whisk well.
Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a cast iron pan, add garlic and sautÃ© till golden brown.
Add spring onion bulbs, mix and sautÃ© for 1 minute.
Add mushrooms, mix and sautÃ© on high heat for 2 minutes. Add salt and crushed black peppercorns and mix well.
Add chicken and mix well. Add beaten eggs, mix and cook for 2 minutes.
Add spring onion greens and mix well.
Take water in a shallow bowl, soak each rice paper sheet for 1 minute.
Remove the rice paper sheet and place on a plate.
Place some chicken mixture in the centre of each rice paper sheet and fold all sides to make parcels. Drizzle some oil and set aside.

Heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in a shallow non-stick pan. Place the prepared parcels (folded sided facing upwards) in it and cook on medium heat for 2 minutes. Flip and cook for 2-3 minutes more. Drain on absorbent paper.
Arrange the parcels on a serving plate, sprinkle white sesame seeds and black sesame seeds and serve hot with chilli oil dip.

title: Fish Fry
description: Fish marinated in masala made in various combination, rolled in spiced semolina-rice flour mixture or in spiced bread crumbs and shallow-fried or deep fried make a wonderful starter. It can also be served with dal and rice to make a memorable meal.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: Take green chilli paste in a bowl, add tamarind pulp, garlic paste, turmeric powder, 1Â½ teaspoons red chilli powder, agri koli masala and salt and mix well.
Add fish pieces and apply the masala well all around.
Spread semolina on a plate, add Â½ teaspoon red chilli powder, salt, coriander leaves and rice flour and mix well.
Heat sufficient oil in a shallow pan. Roll the fish darnes in semolina mixture, place 2 of them in the hot pan and cook on medium heat for 3-4 minutes.
Flip and cook for 3-4 minutes more on the other side. Drain on absorbent paper. Cook the remaining 2 in the same way.
Arrange them on a serving plate and serve hot with onion slices and lemon wedges.

title: French Fries
description: A favourite of kids, French fries that they enjoy all the time. It is relished by adults too especially while watching a favourite movie with the family. Make them at home and enjoy them with the entire family.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: Peel potatoes and cut into batons. Transfer into a bowl of chilled water and set aside for 30 minutes.
Boil sufficient water in a deep pan, add salt and mix well.
Drain the potatoes and add to the boiling water and parboil for 6-7 minutes. Drain and transfer into chilled water.
Drain again and spread on a kitchen towel and pat dry.
Transfer into zip lock bag and freeze for 8-10 hours.
Heat sufficient oil in a kadai. Add frozen fries and deep-fry till golden brown and crisp. Ensure that you do not overcrowd the kadai. Drain on absorbent paper.
Transfer into a bowl and sprinkle salt while still hot and toss well.
Transfer into a serving bowl and serve with tomato ketchup.

title: Garlic Edamame Fried Rice
description: For best results fried rice should be made with cold, one day old rice. With the addition of edamame it becomes a high protein dish. Furthermore garlic, ginger, fresh coriander stems and spring onions add a flavour that is simply irresistible.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: Thin strips of spring onion greens for garnish
ingredients.name: Thin strips of spring onion greens for garnish

steps: Chop spring onion bulbs and greens and keep them separate. Chop fresh red chilli.
Heat oil in a non-stick kadai, add garlic and ginger, mix and sautÃ© till golden brown.
Add coriander stems and fresh red chilli, mix and sautÃ© well.
Add spring onion bulbs, mix and sautÃ© well.
Add carrots and cabbage, mix well and sautÃ© for 2-3 minutes.
Add edamame beans and mix well.

Add salt, cooked rice, crushed black peppercorns, light soy sauce, green chilli sauce and veg stock cube powder and mix well. Cook on medium heat for 3-4 minutes or till the rice is heated thoroughly.
Add spring onion greens and mix well. Drizzle sesame oil and mix.
Transfer into a serving bowl, sprinkle spring onion greens and serve hot.

title: Gongura Prawns
description: Taste of this super spicy prawn dish from Andhra will linger on for a long time. You are sure to want to have it often and when served to your guests they too will appreciate it a lot.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: 1 medium bunch of sorrel (gongura), leaves separated
ingredients.name: medium bunch of sorrel , leaves separated

steps: Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a non-stick kadai, add prawns, Â¼ teaspoon turmeric powder, Â½ teaspoon red chilli powder and salt, mix well and sautÃ© on high heat for 2-3 minutes. Drain and set aside in a bowl.
Heat 2 tablespoons oil in the same kadai, add cumin seeds and once they start to change colour, add garlic, mix and sautÃ© till golden brown.
Add onion, mix and sautÃ© till golden brown. Add green chillies and mix well.
Add Â½ teaspoon turmeric powder, 1 teaspoon red chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, garam masala powder and mix well.
Add sorrel leaves, mix well and sautÃ© for 2-3 minutes. Add Â½ cup water, mix well and cook for 4-5 minutes.
Add sauteed prawns and curry leaves, mix well and cook for 2 minutes.
Transfer into a serving bowl, garnish with slit green chilli and serve hot.

title: Green Moong Halwa
description: Green grams or sabut moong is a powerhouse of plant based proteins, dietary fibre and also has low glycemic index. It can be used to make a variety of savoury and sweet dishes and here we bring you halwa that is sure to win your palate.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: 1Â½ cups soaked and drained whole green gram
ingredients.name: soaked and drained whole green gram

steps: Transfer drained green grams into a grinder jar and grind coarsely.
Heat ghee in a non-stick kadai, add ground green grams and sautÃ© on medium heat for 10-15 minutes, stirring continuously.
Add almonds and pistachios and mix well.
Add milk and 1 cup water, mix well and cook for 15-20 minutes.
Add sugar, mix and cook till sugar dissolves.
Add khoya and green cardamom powder, mix and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Transfer into a serving bowl, garnish with silver varq, sprinkle pistachios, almonds and dried rose petals and serve.

title: Har Gao
description: Har gao is a traditional Cantonese dumpling served as dim sum. Stuffed with prawn mixture and steamed whereby the wrapper becomes somewhat translucent. Perhaps that is why it is also called crystal shrimp dumplings.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: To make the stuffing, take prawns in a bowl, add water chestnuts, salt, white pepper powder, castor sugar and 1 teaspoon blended sesame oil and mix well. Add 2-3 teaspoons cornflour and mix well.

Take wheat starch in another bowl, add Â½ teaspoon blended sesame oil and 160 millilitres hot water and mix.Â Transfer this mixture on a chopping board and knead. Add 12 grams cornflour and knead to a stiff dough.
Roll the dough into a cylinder, cut into even pieces and shape into balls.
Take a piece of muslin cloth and pour some oil over it, place a cleaver over it and press so that it is greased lightly.
Place a dough ball on a chopping board, place the cleaver flat over it and move it in a circular motion to flatten it evenly ensuring it forms a circle.
Heat sufficient water in the lower part of a steamer.

Take the flattened dough, put a spoonful of stuffing in the centre and fold into half, press the edges and form pleats. Cut off extra dough from the flattened edge with the cleaver. Arrange these dumplings in a steamer basket lined with a butter paper. Cover the basket and place on the steamer and steam for 8-10 minutes.

Meanwhile to make the dipping sauce, take dark soy sauce in a small bowl, add garlic, chilli oil and castor sugar and mix well. Add spring onion greens, toasted white sesame seeds and Â½ cup water and mix well.
Arrange the har gaos on a serving platter and serve hot with the dipping sauce.

title: Homemade Ladi Pav
description: Ladi pavs are soft and puffy Indian style bread that is a favourite of almost everyone in Mumbai. Though it is associated with pav bhaji or vada pav or anda bhurji pav, it can be served with any curry both vegetarian and non vegetarian.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: Take refined flour in stand mixer jar, add instant dried yeast, salt, sugar and milk powder and switch on the mixer.
Add 170 milliliters water and process for 10 minutes to form soft dough.
Add butter and knead for 5 minutes more.
Dust some refined flour on the worktop, transfer the dough over it, knead it and roll it into a ball.
Dust some refined flour on a plate, place the dough in it, cover with moist muslin cloth and set aside for 55-60 minutes to prove.
Dust some more refined flour on the worktop, transfer the dough over it, roll into a log and cut into 6 equal portions.
Roll each portion into a ball, place them in a greased burfi tray, cover with a moist muslin cloth and set aside for another 55-60 minutes to prove.
Preheat the air fryer to 180Â° C for 3-4 minutes.
Brush milk on top of each dough ball and place the tray in the air fryer basket. Fit the basket to the air fryer and air fry at 180Â° C for 10-12 minutes.
Brush some melted butter on top of the balls, demould, flip and place them back in the burfi tray.
Fit the basket to the air fryer and air fry at 180Â° C for another 5 minutes.
Bring the basket out and allow to cool. Arrange the ladi pavs on a serving platter and serve.

title: Iced Latte
description: Iced latte is a cold coffee made by mixing espresso shots with cold milk and ice. It is a creamier mixture that has a higher milk to coffee ratio than cold coffee. So enjoy it to the fullest.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: For each portion pour 60 ml espresso in a mason jar. Add Â½ cup hot water and top up with Â¾ cup milk and mix well.
Add 6-8 ice cubes and mix. Serve chilled.

title: Idiyappam
description: Idiyappam also known as string hoppers is a much loved snack in the South Indian states. It consists of rice flour cooked in hot water, kneaded into a soft dough and pressed into noodles on greased banana leaf piece.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: Boil 1Â½ cups water in a non-stick pan, add salt and idiyappam flour and mix well. Sprinkle 2-3 tablespoons water, cover and cook on medium heat for 4-5 minutes.
Transfer into a bowl and knead well to a soft dough.
Divide the mixture into 2 portions and roll into logs.
Grease banana leaf pieces with some oil.
Transfer the logs, one at a time, into a greased chakli press fitted with medium sev attachment and press roundels on the greased banana leaf pieces.
Heat sufficient water in the lower vessel of a steamer, place the leaves in the perforated vessel and steam for 8-10 minutes.
Transfer the roundels onto a serving plate and serve with vegetable stew.

title: Jamun Fuljar Soda
description: Ever tried jamun soda? If not do not waste any time and follow this recipe when jamun are in season.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: Deseed jamuns and put the pulp into a blender jar. Add mint leaves, sugar, squeeze the juice of lemon directly into it. Add salt and blend to a fine puree. Transfer into 4 individual shot glasses.
Take drinking soda in 4 individual highball glasses, and drop each shot glass with the jamun mixture into each highball glass. Serve immediately.

title: Jhinga Hara Pyaaz Tikki
description: These Jhinga Hara Pyaaz Tikkis take the comfort of a classic tikki and elevate it with juicy prawns, fresh spring onions and aromatic spices. Crisp on the outside, soft and juicy inside, every bite balances familiarity with a surprising seafood twist.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: 250 grams medium prawns (jhinga), peeled and deveined
ingredients.name: medium prawns , peeled and deveined

ingredients.raw: Red chilli-garlic chutney to drizzle + for serving
ingredients.name: Red chilli-garlic chutney to drizzle + for serving

steps: Chop spring onion bulbs and greens and keep them separately. Chop prawns.
Put mint leaves in a grinder jar, add coriander leaves, garlic, ginger and green chillies and grind coarsely. Add Â¼ cup water and grind to fine paste.
Heat oil in a shallow non-stick pan, add chopped spring onion bulbs and sautÃ© till translucent.
Add ground paste, turmeric powder, coriander powder, cumin powder and garam masala powder, mix well and sautÃ© for 2 minutes.
Add chopped prawns and salt, mix and saute for 2-3 minutes on high heat.
Add spring onion greens and mix well. Take the pan off the heat and set aside to cool.
Meanwhile, take boiled potatoes in a bowl and mash them.
Add cooked prawn mixture, squeeze lemon, add chaat masala and fresh breadcrumbs and mix well.
Dip your palms in water, divide the mixture into equal portions and roll into balls. Flatten each ball and shape into tikkis and keep them on a plate.
Heat sufficient oil in a non-stick pan. Take cornflour in another plate.
Roll the tikkis in cornflour, place them in the pan and shallow-fry on high heat, flipping sides, till evenly golden on both the sides. Drain on absorbent paper.
Arrange the tikkis on a serving plate, drizzle some red chilli-garlic chutney on top of each tikki, garnish with micro greens and serve hot with red chilli-garlic chutney.

title: Kerala Style Chicken Roast
description: A chicken dish which is typical of Kerala cooking with its bold, fiery taste. Succulent pieces of chicken are slow cooked in coconut oil with a blend of traditional spices. The result â€“ smoky flavour an enticing mix of heat and earthy notes.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: 400 grams boneless chicken leg, cut into1 inch pieces
ingredients.name: boneless chicken leg, cut into1 inch pieces

steps: Heat coconut oil in a non-stick kadai, add dried coconut and sautÃ© till golden brown.
Add curry leaves and green chillies and mix well. Add onion. mix and sautÃ© till golden brown.
Add cumin powder, coriander powder, turmeric powder, garam masala powder, coarse red chilli powder and salt and mix well.
Add chicken, mix and sautÃ© on high heat for 2-3 minutes. Cook on medium heat for 8-10 minutes or till chicken is done.
Transfer onto a serving plate, garnish with fried curry leaves and serve hot with Malabar parotta.

title: Kesar Coconut Laddoo
description: This recipe brings together sweetness of coconut and warm aroma of saffron to create a dessert that feels festive and comforting all at once. Soft, fragrant, and beautifully balanced, each laddoo carries richness of tradition with modern touch.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: Heat a shallow non-stick pan, add saffron and roast on low heat for 30 seconds. Transfer into a mortar and crush with a pestle.
Heat ghee in a non-stick kadai, add coconut and crushed saffron, mix and sautÃ© on medium heat for 4-5 minutes.
Add sugar, mix and cook till sugar dissolves.
Add milk and green cardamom powder, mix and cook till the mixture thickens.
Transfer the mixture onto a plate, spread slightly and allow to cool completely.
Spread desiccated coconut on another plate.
Divide the cooked mixture into equal portions and roll each into a round laddoo. Roll these laddoos in desiccated coconut and arrange them in a serving plate. Serve.

title: Khandvi
description: Khandvi is one of the most popular Gujarati snack. Gram flour batter cooked till thick, spread thinly on thalis, cut into strips and rolled into cylinders. They are then tempered and served â€“ so very delicious that you will not be able to resist.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: Chopped fresh coriander leaves to sprinkle
ingredients.name: Chopped fresh coriander leaves to sprinkle

steps: Take gram flour in a bowl, add refined flour, turmeric powder, red chilli powder, salt, green chilli paste, ginger paste and Â¼ teaspoon asafoetida and mix well.
Add buttermilk gradually and whisk well to get a smooth batter.
Heat a non-stick kadai, add the gram flour mixture and cook on low heat for 15-20 minutes or till the mixture starts to leaves the sides of the pan.
Grease the backside of thalis with oil.
Transfer the mixture on greased thalis and spread evenly all over. Allow to cool and cut into thick strips.
Sprinkle coconut and coriander leaves and roll each strip. Keep the rolls in a serving plate.

To make the tempering, heat oil in a shallow non-stick pan, add mustard seeds and once they start to splutter, add Â¼ teaspoon asafoetida, curry leaves and white sesame seeds and mix well. Pour this tempering over rolled khandvi and serve.

title: Korean Chilli Potatoes
description: A popular street food from Korea, these chilli potatoes are soft, chewy, mushroom-shaped potato dumplings tossed in a savoury and garlicky sauce. They are visually appealing and their glossy texture will appease anyone.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: 2 teaspoons Korean instant noodle spice mix
ingredients.name: Korean instant noodle spice mix

ingredients.raw: 2-3 tablespoons chopped spring onion greens + to sprinkle
ingredients.name: -3 tablespoons chopped spring onion greens + to sprinkle

ingredients.raw: 2-3 teaspoons white sesame seeds + to sprinkle
ingredients.name: -3 teaspoons white sesame seeds + to sprinkle

steps: Cut potatoes into 1-inch pieces.
Boil sufficient water in a deep pan, add salt and potatoes, mix and cook for 12-15 minutes.
Drain the potatoes, cool slightly, mash well and transfer into a bowl. Add salt, cornflour, refined flour and 2 teaspoons oil and mix well to make a soft dough.
Shape the mixture into a log and divide into small portions. Roll each portion into ball.
Press each ball in the centre lightly with a glass bottle to make it look like button.
Boil sufficient water in a deep pan, add salt, 1 teaspoon oil and the potato buttons. Blanch for 4-5 minutes or till the buttons start to float on top.
Take spring onion bulbs in a bowl, add garlic, Korean instant noodle spice mix, spring onion greens and white sesame seeds.
Heat Â¼ cup oil in a pan. Add this hot oil to spring onion mixture.
Drain the potato buttons and add to the spring onion-garlic mixture so that all the buttons are well coated.
Transfer into a serving bowl, sprinkle spring onion greens and white sesame seeds and serve hot.

title: Laal Maas
description: A spicy gem from Rajasthan is Laal Maas. The spices speak for themselves! This rich and fiery curry will take you to the heart of Rajasthan.  The taste is something that you will never forget.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: 750 grams mutton, cut into 2-inch pieces on the bone
ingredients.name: mutton, cut into 2-inch pieces on the bone

steps: Heat mustard oil in a pan till it begins to smoke. Add green cardamoms, cloves, black cardamoms, cinnamon and bay leaf, mix and sautÃ© till fragrant.

Add onions, mix and sautÃ© till translucent. Add mutton, ginger-garlic paste, Mathania chilli paste and salt and mix well. Add Mathania dried red chillies, mix well and sear on high heat for 5-6 minutes.
Meanwhile, take yogurt in a bowl, add turmeric powder, red chilli powder and coriander powder and whisk well. Add Â¼ cup water and mix well.
Add yogurt mixture to the pan and mix well. Cook on high heat for 8-10 minutes or till oil separates.
Rinse the bowl with 2Â½ cups water and add to the pan. Mix well and once the mixture comes to a boil, add salt and mix well. Lower the heat, cover and cook for 45-50 minutes.
Place a bowl with hot coal in the middle of the pan and drizzle 1 tablespoon ghee. Cover immediately for 1 minute.
Remove the cover and take away the bowl with hot coal. Add 2 tablespoons ghee, mix well and cook for 1 minute.
Transfer into a serving bowl, garnish with coriander sprig and serve hot with bajra rotis.

title: Lychee Baked Yogurt
description:
Try this delicious dessert while the lychees are in season. It is so very easy to make yet the end result is irresistible.
This is Sanjeev Kapoor exclusive recipe.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: Preheat oven to 180Âº C.
Peel lychees, remove their seeds and cut the pulp into small pieces.
Take fresh cream in a bowl, add condensed milk, yogurt and chopped lychees and mix till well combined.
Transfer the mixture into individual earthen bowls. Cover with aluminium foil and make a few holes on it.
Arrange the bowls on a baking tray, pour some water in the tray and place it in the preheated oven and bake for 12-15 minutes.
Bring the tray out of the oven and allow the baked yogurt to cool completely. Refrigerate till chilled. Remove the aluminium foil and garnish the baked yogurt with mint sprigs and serve.

title: Lychee Cheesecake
description: Though lychee is a seasonal fruit, there are a number of recipes that you can make with this juicy fruit. Here is a cheesecake that will surely win your heart.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: Arrange some mint leaves in a small silicon Bundt mould. Similarly, arrange the lychees over the mint leaves. Arrange some more mint leaves over the lychees.
Take melted gelatine in a bowl, add castor sugar and mix till it dissolves. Pour this mixture over the lychees mixture and refrigerate for 1 hour.
Take cream cheese in a large bowl, add condensed milk, fresh cream, whipped cream, and whisk till smooth.
Add melted gelatine and mix well. Pour this mixture over the set lychee mixture.
Take digestive biscuits in a zip lock bag and crush them with a rolling pin. Sprinkle it over the cream cheese mixture and press it firmly. Refrigerate this for 1-2 hours or till completely set.
Carefully demould the cheesecake on a serving plate. Slice into wedges and serve.

title: Makhana Laddoo
description: Makhanas are low in calories and fat but rich in plant protein and complex carbohydrates. They can be turned into various dishes and here we have made them into laddoos with dates and nuts that make them very nutritious on medium heat ious that can be beneficial to both children and adults.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: Dried rose petal powder for coating
ingredients.name: Dried rose petal powder for coating

steps: Heat a non-stick pan, add puffed lotus seeds and dry roast on medium heat for 5-6 minutes. Set aside to cool.
Transfer into a food processor and process to a coarse mixture.
Add dates, almonds, pistachios and cashewnuts and process to a coarse mixture.
Transfer the ground mixture onto a plate, add green cardamom powder and mix well.
Divide the mixture into equal portions and roll each into a laddoo.Â Take pistachio powder, dried rose petal powder and desiccared coconut in 3 separate bowls.
Roll some laddoos in pistachio powder, some in dried rose petal powder and remaining in desiccated coconut.
Arrange the laddoos on a serving plate and serve.

title: Mango Boba
description: There must be very few who donâ€™t enjoy mangoes in various ways. Here we decided to give it our own twist by turning it into the most refreshing â€˜mango bobaâ€™ youâ€™ll want to sip again and again.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: Â½ cup tapioca flour + for dusting
ingredients.name: Â½ cup tapioca flour + for dusting

steps: To make boba pearls, heat â…“ cup water in non-stick pan, add castor sugar and cocoa powder, mix well and cook till the sugar dissolves.
Add 2 tablespoons of tapioca starch, mix well and cook till mixture becomes thick.
Place the remaining tapioca starch on the worktop, add the thickened mixture and knead well to a soft dough.
Divide the mixture into small equal portions and roll into balls and keep them in a bowl. Sprinkle tapioca flour over them.
Boil sufficient water in a deep non-stick pan, add the tapioca pearls and cook on medium heat for 10-15 minutes.
Meanwhile to make mango milk, peel and cut mangoes into small pieces and transfer into a blender jar. Add milk and sugar and blend to a smooth mixture and set aside.
Strain the tapioca pearls and dip them into chilled water.
Drain these pearls and put 2 tablespoons in each serving glass. Add crushed ice and pour the mango milk and mix well, serve chilled.

title: Mango Lassi And Mango Martini
description: To celebrate the last few mangoes of the season, here are recipes of Mango Lassi and Mango Martini. Do make it at home and let us know how it turned out. Mango Lassi is a beloved drink during the hot summer months and is a delicious way to cool down and quench thirst.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: 2 cups chopped ripe alphonso mango + to garnish
ingredients.name: chopped ripe alphonso mango + to garnish

ingredients.raw: 12-16 seedless red dates, soaked in warm water for 20 minutes and drained
ingredients.name: -16 seedless red dates, soaked in warm water for 20 minutes and drained

ingredients.raw: 4 pinches of saffron strands + to garnish
ingredients.name: pinches of saffron strands + to garnish

steps: To make one portion of lassi, put Â½ cup chopped mango in a blender jar, add Â½ cup yogurt and 3-4 dates and blend. Add ice cubes and a pinch of saffron strands and continue to blend to a fine mixture. Pour this into a serving glass, garnish with chopped mangoes, few saffron strands and almonds. Serve immediately. Use up the remaining ingredients similarly.
To make martini mix salt and red chilli powder in a plate. Apply some lemon juice on the rim of a martini glass and press it over salt-red chilli mixture.

To make one portion, put 2 tablespoons mango pulp in a shaker, add ice cubes, a few mint leaves, squeeze the juice of Â½ lemon directly into it, add Â½ teaspoon sugar substitute and Â¼ cup drinking soda and shake it lightly till well combined. Strain into the prepared glass and garnish with a mint leave. Serve immediately. Use up the remaining ingredients similarly.

title: Masala Kaju
description: This dish may be simple and easy to make but it makes a fantastic starter. Crisp and flavourful  cashewnuts cooked this way are simply divine to taste and can be enjoyed by both adults and children.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: Â½ teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder
ingredients.name: Â½ teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder

steps: Preheat the air fryer at 150Â° C for 5 minutes.
Take cashewnuts in a bowl, add Kashmiri red chilli powder, turmeric powder, chaat masala, ghee and salt and mix well.
Spread the cashewnuts evenly in air fry basket, fit the basket to the air fryer and air fry for 15 minutes.
Bring the basket out of the air fryer and allow to cool. Transfer into a serving bowl and serve.

title: Masala Mathri
description: A crisp snack is a favourite of most of us. But if and when we want to avoid deep-fried item then here is a delicious snack that will satiate your palate for sure.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: Â¼ cup whole wheat flour (atta) + for dusting
ingredients.name: Â¼ cup whole wheat flour  + for dusting

steps: Take pearl millet flour in a bowl, add whole wheat flour, ghee, white sesame seeds, red chilli powder, turmeric powder, crushed coriander seeds, carom seeds, coriander leaves and salt and mix well.
Add 5 tablespoons warm water and knead to a soft dough.
Preheat the air fryer at 180Â° C for 5 minutes.
Dust some whole wheat flour on the worktop and roll the dough into a thin sheet. Dock it all over and cut into small roundels with a round cookie cutter.
Arrange in the air fryer basket, fit the basket to the air fryer and air fry for 10 minutes.
Bring the basket out and let the mathris cool down to room temperature.
Transfer them onto a serving bowl and serve.

title: Milk Bread
description:
This classic Japanese milk bread wins many a heart with its unique look and soft and fluffy
texture.  So make it at home in an air fryer keeping in mind that the dough should neither 
be soft nor dry nor stiff.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: Take refined flour in a dough kneading machine, add salt, castor sugar, instant dry yeast, milk and 60 millilitres water and knead to a soft dough.
Transfer the dough onto the worktop and knead with your hand well. Keep it in a bowl, cover with a damp cloth till it doubles in volume.
Divide the dough into 3 equal portions. Spray some oil on the worktop and roll each portion into a rectangle.

Fold in two sides to the middle and further roll from one end to the other to resemble a log. Â Arrange the 3 logs side by side in a greased loaf tin, cover with a damp cloth and set aside for 1 hour to prove.Preheat air fryer at 165Â°C for 2-3 minutes.
Brush the logs with milk. Keep the loaf tin in the air fryer basket, fit it to the air fryer and air fry for 16-17 minutes.
Bring loaf tin out, baste the rolls with butter and allow to cool.
Demould and keep the bread logs on a serving platter and serve.

title: Milk Bread Tangzhong Style
description: Tangzhong is a Chinese style bread made with cooked roux of flour and milk and bread flour to make a bread which is oh so soft and flurry and stays so for long.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: To prepare roux, heat milk in a non-stick pan, add refined flour, whisk well and cook on low heat till mixture thickens. Set aside to cool completely.
To make the dough, take milk in a bowl, add instant dry yeast, castor sugar, salt and butter.
Add the prepared roux and whisk well. Add bread flour and knead to soft dough.
Cover with a moist muslin cloth and set aside for 1 hour or until the dough doubles in size.
Divide the dough into 3 equal portions and roll into balls. Cover with moist muslin cloth and set aside for 10-15 minutes.
Spray some oil on the worktop, roll each ball into a rectangle, fold all sides and roll tightly. Pinch the edges.
Transfer all rolled balls in a greased loaf tin, cover with a moist muslin cloth and set aside for 30-40 minutes.
Preheat the oven at 165Â° C.
Apply milk wash over the dough balls and place the loaf tin in the preheated oven and bake for 30-35 minutes.
Brush butter on top. Allow to cool and use as required.

title: Moogacho Ros
description: A classic Goan curry made with sprouted moong cooked in a coconut and roasted spices gravy, Moogache ros is a highly flavourful dish that can be best enjoyed with steamed rice.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: Heat a shallow non-stick pan, add dried red chillies, coriander seeds, peppercorns and fenugreek seeds, mix and dry roast for 2-3 minutes. Allow to cool.
Transfer the roasted ingredients into a grinder jar, add coconut, roughly chopped onion, tamarind, turmeric powder and Â¾ cup water and grind to fine paste.
Heat ghee in a non-stick kadai, add mustard seeds and once they start to splutter, add asafoetida and curry leaves, mix and sautÃ© for a few seconds.
Add chopped onion, salt and green chillies, mix and sautÃ© till onion turns translucent. Add sprouted green gram, mix and sautÃ© for 2-3 minutes.
Add 2 cups water, cover and cook for 5-6 minutes. Add ground paste and tirphal and mix well. Cover and cook on medium heat for 8-10 minutes.
Add jaggery and coriander leaves, mix and cook till jaggery dissolves.
Transfer into a serving bowl, garnish with coriander sprig and serve hot with steamed rice.

title: Moong Dal Pakoda
description: Vegetables like cabbage, spinach and some aromatics added to ground moong dal make the pakodas not only more nutritious but super delicious too.  Here we bring you a recipe of the pakodas that even weight watchers can enjoy because they are air fried.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
main_ingredients: Split skinless green gram (dhuli moong dal)

ingredients.raw: 1 cup split skinless green gram (dhuli moong dal), soaked for 30 minutes and drained
ingredients.name: split skinless green gram , soaked for 30 minutes and drained

steps: Put split green gram in a grinder jar, add ginger, garlic and green chili and grind to a coarse paste.
Transfer the paste into a bowl, add onion, cabbage, spinach, carom seeds, asafoetida, turmeric powder, white sesame seeds, salt, chaat masala, baking soda and 1 tablespoon oil and mix well.
Preheat the air fryer at 180Â° C for 10 minutes. Line the air fryer basket with butter paper and brush with oil.
Place small portions of the mixture in the basket, fit it to the air fryer and air fry for 8 minutes.
Bring the basket out, flip the pakodas, brush some more oil over them and air fry for 7 minutes more.
Bring the basket out of the air fryer, transfer the pakodas into a serving bowl and serve hot with tomato ketchup.
tags: Split skinless green gram (dhuli moong dal)

title: Moong Palak Puri
description: These puris are made with whole wheat flour and semolina kneaded with ground sprouted moong and spinach, ginger and green chillies. Their taste is as great as the lovely green colour.
author: Neena Murdeshwar

ingredients.raw: 1 tablespoon oil + for greasing + for deep frying
ingredients.name: oil + for greasing + for deep frying

steps: Put sprouted green grams into a grinder jar, add blanched spinach, ginger, green chillies and Â½ cup water and grind to a fine paste.
Transfer the ground paste into a mixing bowl, add carom seeds, salt, turmeric powder, semolina and whole wheat flour and knead to a stiff dough.
Add 1 tablespoon oil and knead again. Cover with a moist muslin cloth and set aside for 10-15 minutes.
Divide the dough into small equal portions and roll into balls. Grease the worktop with some oil and roll out the balls into semi thick puris.
Heat sufficient oil in a kadai. Slide in the puris, one at a time and deep-fry till golden brown and puffed. Drain on an absorbent paper.
Arrange the puris on a serving platter and serve hot with pickle.

title: Moong Poriyal
description: This Sprouted Moong Poriyal brings a fresh South Indian twist to everyday sprouts with a fragrant coconut tempering, curry leaves, and gentle spice. Light, wholesome, and full of flavour, this dish proves that healthy food doesnâ€™t have to be boring.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
main_ingredients: Split skinless black gram (dhuli urad dal)

ingredients.raw: 1 teaspoon split skinless black gram (dhuli urad dal)
ingredients.name: split skinless black gram

steps: Heat coconut oil in a non-stick kadai, add asafoetida, mustard seeds and split black gram, mix and once the mustard seeds begin to splutter, add dried red chillies, green chillies and curry leaves, mix and sautÃ© for 1 minute.
Add sprouted green gram and salt, mix and cook for 6-8 minutes on medium heat.
Add scraped coconut and mix well.
Transfer into a serving bowl and serve hot.
tags: Split skinless black grams (dhuli urad dal)

title: Moong Soya Tikki
description: When two protein rich ingredients meet the result is not only a healthy dish but also a tasty one. Sabut moong and soya mince and mixed with some aromatics, shaped into tikkis and cooked to perfection.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: 1Â½ cups boiled sprouted green gram (ankurit moong)
ingredients.name: boiled sprouted green gram

ingredients.raw: 3 medium potatoes, boiled, peeled and mashed
ingredients.name: medium potatoes, boiled, peeled and mashed

steps: Heat oil in a shallow non-stick pan, add crushed ginger-garlic and sautÃ© for 30 seconds.
Add green chillies and onion and sautÃ© till onion turns golden brown.
Add soya keema and salt and mix well.
Add turmeric powder, red chilli powder, garam masala powder, cumin powder and coriander powder and mix well.
Take sprouted green gram in a bowl and mash lightly. Add cooked soya mince mixture and mix well.
Add coriander leaves and mashed potatoes and mix well.
Divide the mixture into equal portions and roll into balls. Flatten each ball to shape into tikkis.
Dip the tikkis in refined flour slurry and roll in panko breadcrumbs and keep them on a plate.
Heat sufficient oil in a shallow non-stick pan. Place the tikkis and shallow-fry, turning sides, till evenly golden brown and crisp on both the sides. Drain on absorbent paper.
Transfer them onto a serving plate and serve hot with tomato ketchup.

title: Moong Sprout Kadhi
description: Kadhi is a comfort food which is popular pan India. It is made in various ways and here we have made it healthier by adding sprouted moong.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: Â½ cup sprouted green gram (ankurit moong)
ingredients.name: Â½ cup sprouted green gram

steps: Take gram flour in a bowl, add yogurt and whisk till there are no lumps. Add 2Â½ cups water and mix well.
Heat 2 tablespoons ghee in a non-stick kadai, add cumin seeds and once they start to change colour, add black peppercorns, cinnamon and cloves, mix and sautÃ© till fragrant.
Add yogurt mixture, salt and sugar and mix well.
Add 1 cup water, mix well and cook on low heat for 4-5 minutes.
Add ginger and sprouted green gram, mix and cook for 8-10 minutes.

To make the second tempering, heat remaining ghee in a small non-stick pan. Break red chillies and add to the pan. Add turmeric powder, red chilli powder and asafoetida, mix and sautÃ© for a few seconds.
Add this tempering to the yogurt mixture and mix. Add coriander leaves, mix and bring the mixture to a boil.
Transfer into a serving bowl and serve hot with steamed rice.

title: Murauri
description: An iconic dish from Bihar, murauri is a light and crisp radish puri which you will find light while eating but there will be no compromise when it comes to flavour.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: Oil for deep frying + for greasing
ingredients.name: Oil for deep frying + for greasing

ingredients.raw: Rassewale aloo ki sabzi for serving
ingredients.name: Rassewale aloo ki sabzi for serving

steps: Heat 2 cups water in a kadai, add carom seeds, onion seeds, ginger, red chilli flakes, salt and radish and cook for 8-10 minutes or till the water reduces to half of its original volume.
Add rice flour and mix well. Take the pan off the heat, cover and set aside for 10 minutes.
Transfer the radish mixture into a bowl, add coriander leaves and knead to a smooth dough.Divide the dough into equal portions and roll into balls.
Heat sufficient oil in a kadai.
Grease the rolling board with some oil and roll each ball into a thick disc. Cut with a round cookie cutter.
Gently slide in the discs, one at a time, into hot oil and deep-fry till light golden and crisp. Drain on absorbent paper.
Transfer the murauri onto a serving plate and serve hot with rassewale aloo ki sabzi.

title: Mushroom Soup With Almonds
description: This hearty and creamy mushroom soup is rich and full of umami flavour.  Almonds also lend their unique flavour and texture and it is sure to become your familyâ€™s favourite.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: Blanched, peeled and slivered almonds for garnish
ingredients.name: Blanched, peeled and slivered almonds for garnish

ingredients.raw: Toasted sourdough bread slices for serving
ingredients.name: Toasted sourdough bread slices for serving

steps: Heat butter in a deep non-stick pan, add onion and sautÃ© till translucent.
Add mushrooms, mix and sautÃ© for 3-4 minutes.
Add salt, white pepper powder and almonds and mix well.
Add almonds, mix well, cover and cook for 2-3 minutes. Take the pan off the heat and allow to cool completely.
Transfer into a grinder jar and grind to semi-fine paste.
Transfer into the same pan. Rinse the jar with Â½ cup water, add to the pan and mix well. Add milk, mix well and bring it to a boil. Cover and rest for 2-3 minutes.
Transfer into serving bowls, sprinkle some white pepper powder, garnish with almond slivers and serve hot with toasted sourdough bread slices.

title: Mutton Siddu
description: Siddu is a popular food in Himachal Pradesh. It is a staple during the harsh winter months, because it provides warmth and sustenance. Apparently it was made and carried by nomadic shepherds to fuel long, gruelling journeys.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: Â½ teaspoon dried fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi) powder
ingredients.name: Â½ teaspoon dried fenugreek leaves  powder

steps: Take fresh yeast in a bowl, add castor sugar and 3 tablespoons lukewarm water. Set aside to prove for 10 minutes.

Take whole wheat flour in a mixing bowl, make a well in the centre, pour yeast mixture into it. Add salt and â…“ cup water and knead to soft dough. Cover with a moist muslin cloth and set aside for 1 hour.
To make the stuffing, heat mustard oil in a pan till it begins to smoke, add garlic, ginger and green chillies, mix and sautÃ© till garlic turns golden brown.
Add onion, mix and sautÃ© till golden brown. Add tomato and salt, mix and saute till tomato turns pulpy.
Add turmeric powder, red chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin and dried mango powder and mix well.
Add mutton mince, mix well and sear on high heat for 4-5 minutes.
Add 1Â½ cups water, garam masala powder and green cardamom powder and mix well. Cover with a lid and cook on low heat for 20-25 minutes.
Add coriander leaves and dried fenugreek leaves powder and mix well.
Add walnuts, mix well and cook on medium heat till all the moisture dries.
Transfer onto a plate and let it cool completely.
Heat sufficient water in a steamer.

Divide the dough into equal portions and roll into balls. Roll out each ball into a small disc, place some mutton mixture on one side, fold the other side over it and pinch the edges to seal. This a siddu.
Arrange these in the steamer basket, place it in the steamer, cover and steam for 10-12 minutes.
Remove them from the steamer, slice and place on serving platter. Drizzle ghee and serve hot with green chutney.

title: Noodle Salad
description: A wholesome salad made with rice noodles and some vegetables mixed together with a flavourful and nourishing dressing. Have it once and you will want to have it often.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: Â½ medium raw papaya, peeled and seeded
ingredients.name: Â½ medium raw papaya, peeled and seeded

ingredients.raw: 1 teaspoon white sesame seeds + to sprinkle
ingredients.name: white sesame seeds + to sprinkle

ingredients.raw: 2-3 tablespoons chopped spring onion greens + to sprinkle
ingredients.name: -3 tablespoons chopped spring onion greens + to sprinkle

steps: Shred English cucumber, carrot, raw papaya and purple cabbage.
Take rice noodles in a bowl, add sufficient hot water and set aside for 2-3minutes.
Drain and add to chilled water. Drain and set aside.

To make the dressing, take peanut butter in another bowl. Add garlic juice, ginger juice, red chilli flakes, soy sauce, blended sesame oil, rice wine vinegar, white sesame seeds, black sesame seeds and salt and whisk well. Add honey and mix well.
Arrange the drained rice noodles on a serving plate, add English cucumber, purple cabbage, raw papaya, carrot, roasted peanuts, mixed capsicums, spring onion bulbs and spring onion greens.
Drizzle dressing and mix well.
Transfer into a serving bowl, sprinkle spring onion greens, parsley and white sesame seeds and serve.

title: Osaman
description: A classic Gujarati soup which is light, nutritious and lightly spiced that is extremely comforting to have on a cold night. Made with the water in which sabut moong is boiled and a few spices to add flavour.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: 1 cup whole green gram (sabut moong), soaked for 6-8 hours and drained
ingredients.name: whole green gram , soaked for 6-8 hours and drained

steps: Heat a deep non-stick pan, add drained whole green gram, 5-6 cups water and salt and mix well. Cook on medium heat for 25-30 minutes or till the green gram is completely cooked, discarding the scum that rises to the top. Strain the water into a jar and set aside. Reserve the whole green gram to use in another dish.
Heat oil in a non-stick kadai, add cinnamon and cloves, mix and sautÃ© till fragrant.
Add green chillies and mustard seeds and once the seeds start to splutter, add cumin seeds, asafoetida and curry leaves, mix and sautÃ© for 30 seconds.
Lower the heat, add turmeric powder and mix well. Add the strained water, salt and coriander leaves, mix and boil the mixture for 6-8 minutes.
Transfer into a serving bowl and serve hot.

title: Palak Chole
description: Chole is one of the most popular dishes of Punjabi cuisine. Palak too is an ingredient which is used in various ways in this cuisine. Here we have combined the two to make an absolutely delicious dish that will satiate most foodies.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: Â½ teaspoon dried fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi) powder
ingredients.name: Â½ teaspoon dried fenugreek leaves  powder

steps: Boil sufficient water in a pan, add spinach leaves and blanch for 30 seconds. Drain and dip into chilled water. Drain, squeeze excess water and transfer the spinach into a grinder jar. Add Â½ cup water and grind to fine paste.
Heat 2 tablespoons ghee in a pan, add cumin seeds and once they start to change colour, add 1Â½ tablespoons garlic, mix and sautÃ© till golden brown.
Add green chillies, mix and sautÃ© for 30 seconds. Add onions, mix and sautÃ© till golden brown.
Add ground paste and mix well. Add coriander powder and garam masala powder and mix well.
Add boiled chickpeas, salt and dried fenugreek leaves powder and mix well.
Add chickpea cooking water, mix well and cook for 4-5 minutes on medium heat.
Add butter and fresh cream and mix well.
Heat 2 tablespoons ghee in a small non-stick pan, add 1 tablespoon garlic and sautÃ© till golden brown. Add dried red chillies and mix well.
Transfer the palak chole into serving bowl and pour the prepared tempering on top. Drizzle fresh cream and serve hot with paranthe.

title: Paneer Bhurji
description: There are some recipes that become everyoneâ€™s favourite for they are simple, comforting and full of flavour. They are also easy and quick to make. Paneer bhurji is one of them. It can be enjoyed with paranthe or even pavs.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: Heat a pan, add gram flour and dry roast on medium heat for 4-5 minutes or till fragrant.
Take yogurt in a bowl, add roasted gram flour, Â¼ teaspoon turmeric powder, Â½ teaspoon red chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, salt and garam masala powder and mix well.
Add dried fenugreek leaves and mix well.
Heat oil and butter in a pan, add cumin seeds and once they start to change colour, add ginger, garlic and green chillies, mix and sautÃ© till golden brown.
Add onions, mix and sautÃ© till golden brown. Add tomato and mix well. Add salt, mix and saute till the tomato turns pulpy.
Add Â¼ teaspoon turmeric powder and Â¼ teaspoon red chilli powder and mix well.
Add yogurt mixture and mix well. Add Â½ cup water and cook till oil separates.
Grate cottage cheese directly into the pan, mix well and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Add coriander leaves and mix well. Transfer in a serving bowl, garnish with coriander sprig and serve hot.

title: Paneer Butter Masala
description: Paneer cubes floating in a rich and creamy tomato sauce is something to die for. Butter masala is now popular the world over â€“ like chicken butter masala, paneer butter masala and mixed vegetable butter masala which satiate the most discerning palate.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: 300 grams cottage cheese (paneer), cut into 1-inch cubes
ingredients.name: cottage cheese , cut into 1-inch cubes

ingredients.raw: Â½ teaspoon dried fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi) powder
ingredients.name: Â½ teaspoon dried fenugreek leaves  powder

steps: Heat 2 tablespoons oil and 1 tablespoon butter in a pan. Add green cardamoms, cloves, cinnamon, bay leaf, caraway seeds and dried red chillies, mix and sautÃ© till fragrant.
Add slied onion and fresh coriander stems, mix and saute till onion is translucent.
Add garlic, ginger, sliced tomatoes and cashewnuts and mix well.
Add salt, mix well and saute for 4-5 minutes.
Add 2 cups water, mix well and cook on medium heat for 10-12 minutes or till the tomatoes turn pulpy.
Allow to cool slightly, transfer into grinder jar and grind into a fine paste.
Heat 2 tablespoons oil and 1 tablespoon butter in a pan, add cumin seeds and once they start to change colour, add onions and sautÃ© till light golden brown.
Add green chillies, chopped tomatoes, mix well and sautÃ© for 4-5 minutes.
Add turmeric powder, red chilli powder, coriander powder and salt and mix well. Add 1 cup water, mix well and cook on medium heat for 4-5 minutes or till the tomatoes turn pulpy.
Add ground paste and mix. Rinse the grinder jar with 1Â½ cups water and add to the pan. Mix well and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Add dried fenugreek leaves powder, honey, 4 tablespoons butter and fresh cream, mix well and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Add cottage cheese cubes, mix with light hands and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Transfer into a serving plate, drizzle fresh cream, garnish with a fresh coriander sprig and serve hot with naan.

title: Paneer Moong Toast
description: Paneer has this wonderful quality of mixing well with most ingredients. Here it is mixed with boiled sabut moong along with some other spices to be used as a topping on bread . They are then shallow fried to a crisp perfection to make a delightful snack.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: 1Â½ cups boiled whole green gram (sabut moong)
ingredients.name: boiled whole green gram

ingredients.raw: 2 tablespoons finely chopped fresh coriander leaves
ingredients.name: finely chopped fresh coriander leaves

steps: Take green gram in a bowl and mash well. Add cottage cheese, onion, tomato, coriander leaves, ginger, green chillies, turmeric powder, red chilli powder, garam masala powder, dried mango powder and salt and mix well.
Take bread slices and spread the green gram mixture evenly. Sprinkle white sesame seeds on top and press lightly.
Heat a non-stick tawa and drizzle some oil. Place 2 slices (mixture side facing down) at a time and cook on medium heat for 5-6 minutes.
Flip and cook for 4-5 minutes or till the underside is crisp.
