Place toasts on the worktop and cut each slice into 4 small triangles, place them on a serving platter and serve hot with green chutney and tomato ketchup.

title: Patra
description: A delicious snack made with Colocasia leaves very popular in Gujarat as Patra, in Maharashtra as Alu Wadi and in Karnataka as Patrado. The leaves are layered with a spicy gram flour mixture or coconut-based mixture, rolled into logs and steamed.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: Chopped fresh coriander leaves to sprinkle
ingredients.name: Chopped fresh coriander leaves to sprinkle

steps: Trim the hard vein on the back side of colocasia leaves. Roll rolling pin all over the veins.

To make the mixture, take tamarind water in a bowl, add jaggery, green chilli paste, ginger paste, asafoetida, turmeric powder, red chilli powder, coriander powder, garam masala powder and salt and mix well. Add gram flour and mix well.

Place a leaf on the worktop, shiny side facing downwards. Â Apply the paste evenly all over. Place another leaf over it but with its tapering end in the opposite direction of the first one. Â Apply paste all over this. Place another leaf on top and spread the mixture evenly all over.
Fold in the side edges and gently roll into a cylinder. Apply paste to seal whenever required. Similarly make one more roll.
Heat sufficient water in a deep pan and place a steamer basket over it. Grease the basket with little oil and place the prepared rolls in it. Cover with the lid and steam for 25-30 minutes.
Bring the rolls out, allow to cool completely and cut into thick slices.
Heat 2-3 tablespoons oil in a shallow non-stick pan. Â Add mustard seeds and once they start to splutter, add white sesame seeds and curry leaves and mix well.
Add the patra slices and toss well. Cook for 2-3 minutes.
Transfer onto a serving plate, sprinkle scraped coconut and coriander leaves and serve.

title: Pecan Banana Loaf
description: This Pecan Banana Bread is rich, nutty and its taste is simply superb so much so that it will linger on long after you have eaten it. It is easy to make and the end result is a golden fragrant loaf that can be served any time.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: Â½ cup chopped pecan nuts + to sprinkle
ingredients.name: Â½ cup chopped pecan nuts + to sprinkle

ingredients.raw: Â¾ cup chopped dark chocolate + to sprinkle
ingredients.name: Â¾ cup chopped dark chocolate + to sprinkle

steps: Preheat oven to 180Â° C.
Take brown sugar in a grinder jar, add castor sugar, melted butter, sour cream, egg and vanilla essence.
Peel bananas and add grind to fine mixture.
Transfer this mixture into a bowl. Sift refined flour, baking soda and baking powder directly in the bowl.
Add salt and mix the ingredients well.
Add pecan nuts and chocolate and mix well.
Transfer the batter into a piping bag and pipe into greased rectangular aluminium loaf mould.
Sprinkle pecan nuts and dark chocolate, keep the mould in the preheated oven and bake for 20 minutes.
Bring the mould out, allow to cool and unmould the loaf. Â Slice and place them on a serving plate and serve.

title: Pesto Chicken Ravioli
description: The raviolis may require a little effort to make but the end result is well worth it. Flavourful chicken mince mixture and pesto sauce make this dish extra delicious and special.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: Extra virgin olive oil to drizzle
ingredients.name: Extra virgin olive oil to drizzle

steps: Take refined flour on worktop, add fine semolina and salt and mix well.
Make a well in the centre, add egg yolks and egg whites and knead to make a stiff dough.Â Transfer into a zip lock bag and set aside for 15-20 minutes.
Put chicken mince in a grinder jar, add parsley, salt, crushed black peppercorns and fresh cream and grind to fine mixture. Transfer this mixture into a piping bag and set aside.
Dust some refined flour on the surface and on pasta machine. Roll out the dough into thin sheet through it.
Spread the dough sheet on the work top, pipe the chicken mince mixture at equal distance on one side of the sheet, apply water and fold.
Cut into small squares and pinch the edges to seal. These are raviolis.
Heat sufficient water in a deep pan, add the raviolis and cook for 5-6 minutes.
Meanwhile, heat a pan. Drain the raviolis and add to the pan. Â Add pesto sauce, cherry tomatoes and fresh cream and mix well.
Transfer onto a serving plate, garnish with parmesan shavings, basil sprigs, drizzle extra virgin olive oil and serve hot.

title: Phodshi Chana Dal Bhaji
description: Phodishi is a wild green leafy vegetable that is popular in Maharashtra. Cooked with chana dal and some simple condiments, it transforms into a highly flavourful dish that is best enjoyed with bhakris.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: Â½ cup cooked split Bengal gram (chana dal)
ingredients.name: Â½ cup cooked split Bengal gram

ingredients.raw: Â¼ cup scraped fresh coconut + to sprinkle
ingredients.name: Â¼ cup scraped fresh coconut + to sprinkle

steps: Discard the white part of the stem and chop the leaves.
Take water in a bowl, add salt and mix well. Add the leaves, mix well and set aside for 10-15 minutes.
Put green chillies and garlic cloves into a mortar and crush coarsely with a pestle.
Heat oil in a pan, add crushed garlic-green chillies and sautÃ© till the garlic turns golden.
Add onions and salt, mix and sautÃ© till golden brown.
Add turmeric powder and red chilli powder and mix well.
Add split Bengal gram and mix well. Add chopped phodshi and mix well. Cover the pan with a lid and pour some water in the lid. Cook on medium heat for 8-10 minutes.
Remove the lid and add scraped coconut and mix well.
Transfer into a serving bowl, sprinkle some more scraped coconut on top and serve hot with bhakris.

title: Pink Dahi Bhalle
description:
In this dish you can enjoy a classic Indian favourite with a colourful and enjoyable twist. 
Soak the bhalles in pink coloured yogurt wherein the beautiful colour is got from beetroot puree.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
main_ingredients: Split skinless black grams (dhuli urad dal)

ingredients.raw: 1Â½ cups split skinless black grams (dhuli urad dal), soaked for 3-4 hours and drained
ingredients.name: split skinless black grams , soaked for 3-4 hours and drained

ingredients.raw: Date and tamarind chutney, as required
ingredients.name: Date and tamarind chutney, as required

steps: Put split black gram in a grinder jar, add green chillies, ginger, salt and Â½ cup water and grind into a fine and thick batter.

Transfer the batter into a bowl and beat it with an electric hand beater for 4-5 minutes. To test if the batter is ready drop a spoonful of batter in a bowl of cold water. If it floats then it is ready.
Heat sufficient oil in a kadai.
Take another bowl and pour sufficient water in it. Add asafoetida and mix well.

Dampen your fingers, take small portions of the batter and drop directly into hot oil and deep-fry, ensuring that you donâ€™t overcrowd the kadai. When they turn golden, drain on absorbent paper. These are bhalles.
Dip these bhalles in the asafoetida flavoured water for 8-10 minutes.
Take beetroot puree in a deep bowl, add yogurt, add black salt, salt and powdered sugar and mix well.
Add Â½ cup chilled water and mix well and refrigerate for 30-40 minutes.
Take the bowl of yogurt out of the refrigerator. Drain the bhalle from cold water and squeeze them lightly to remove excess water and add to the chilled yogurt.

Transfer 2 bhalles in each serving bowl, drizzle date and tamarind chutney, green chutney, sprinkle black salt, roasted cumin powder, fresh pomegranate pearls and garnish with fresh coriander sprigs. Serve immediately.
tags: Split skinless black gram (dhuli urad dal)

title: Pita Bread
description: Pita bread is popular middle eastern flatbread that is leavened with yeast and baked. Pita is also known to have an interior pocket that can be stuffed with falafel, hummus, tzatziki or grilled chicken or a tangy vegetables salad.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: Take instant dried yeast in a bowl, add sugar, refined flour, salt and 1 tablespoon oil.
Add 130 millilitres water and knead to soft dough. Cover with a moist muslin cloth and set aside for 1 hour.
Dust some refined flour on the worktop, place the dough over it and roll it into a log. Divide into 16 equal portions and roll each portion into a ball.
Dust some more refined flour on the worktop, roll the balls into oval shape. Cover again with a moist muslin cloth and set aside for 15 minutes.
Preheat the air fryer at 200Â°C for 5 minutes.
Arrange the prepared pita breads, a few at a time, in the air fryer basket, fit the basket to the air fryer and air fry for 5 minutes.
Bring the basket out of the air fryer and let the pita breads cool. Arrange them on a serving platter and serve with hummus.

title: Potato Wedges
description: Crispy on the outside, soft on the inside these wedges are the kind of snack you keep reaching for without even realising it. That golden crunch followed by warm, comforting potato makes every bite deeply satisfying, minus the heaviness of frying.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: Wash and scrub potatoes clean. Halve each potato lengthwise, then cut into each half into 3-4 wedges.
Heat sufficient water in a deep pan, add potatoes and parboil for 7-8 minutes. Drain and pat dry on a kitchen towel.
Take Kashmiri red chilli powder in a bowl, add dried basil, dried thyme, dried oregano, salt, onion powder, garlic powder, crushed black peppercorns and cornflour and mix well.
Add the potatoes and mix well. Make sure each wedge is nicely coated with the spice mix.
Preheat the air fryer at 200Â° C for 2-3 minutes.

Spray some oil in air fryer basket and arrange the wedges in a single layer. Spray some more oil on top. Â Fit the basket to the air fryer and air fry for 12-15 minutes, shaking once after 8-10 minutes.
Bring the basket out, transfer the potato wedges onto a serving plate and serve hot with tomato ketchup.

title: Prawn Curry With Raw Mangoes
description: Delightful Prawn Curry with Raw Mangoes is a traditional south Indian prawn recipe cooked in creamy coconut gravy. The tangy flavour imparted by the raw mangoes and the classic flavour of coconut oil, make this curry super tasty.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: 1 inch ginger, cut into thin strips
ingredients.name: inch ginger, cut into thin strips

ingredients.raw: 1 teaspoon + 1 tablespoon Kashmiri red chilli powder
ingredients.name: + 1 tablespoon Kashmiri red chilli powder

steps: Heat 2Â½ cups water in earthenware pot, add raw mangoes, curry leaves, ginger, green chillies, turmeric powder,1 teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder and salt and mix well. Cover and cook for 8-10 minutes.
Put scraped coconut in a grinder jar, add 1 tablespoon Kashmiri red chilli powder, shallots and Â½ cup water and grind to fine paste.
Add prawns to the raw mango mixture and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Add ground paste, rinse the grinder jar with 2-3 tablespoons water and add to the pot. Mix well and once the mixture comes to a boil, switch the heat off and keep the pot covered.
For tempering, heat coconut oil in a small pan, add mustard seeds and fenugreek seeds. Once the mustard seeds start to splutter, add shallots and fry till the shallots turn golden brown.
Add red chilli flakes and curry leaves, switch off the heat and mix well.
Add this tempering to the curry, cover immediately and rest for 10-15 minutes. Mix well.
Transfer into a serving bowl and serve hot with matta rice.

title: Prawn Patty
description: A great snack idea for all prawn lovers. Itâ€™s simple and easy to make but absolutely delicious. Prawns ground with a few aromatics, shaped into patties and deep-fried till crisp and golden.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: Put prawns in a food processor jar, add garlic cloves, coriander stems and salt and process to coarse mixture.
Transfer the mixture into a bowl, add sriracha sauce, coriander leaves, crushed red chillies-ginger and white pepper powder and mix well.
Break eggs in another bowl and beat well. Spread panko breadcrumbs on a plate.
Wet your palms with some water, take small portions of the mixture and roll into balls. Flatten each ball slightly and dip in beaten eggs. Roll in panko breadcrumbs and set aside on a large plate.
Heat sufficient oil in a kadai, gently slide the patties into it and deep-fry till golden brown and crisp. Drain on an absorbent paper.
Arrange them on a serving plate and serve hot with sweet chilli sauce.

title: Rajgire Ka Parantha
description: Soft, flavorful rajgira parathas made with mashed potatoes and spices, perfect for a wholesome, gluten-free meal. This is Sanjeev Kapoor exclusive recipe.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: 1 tablespoon ghee + for greasing and cooking
ingredients.name: ghee + for greasing and cooking

steps: Take amaranth flour in a bowl, add rock salt, roasted cumin powder, coriander leaves and ginger-green chilli paste.
Mash the potatoes and add to the bowl and mix. Add ghee and mix well. Add Â½ cup warm water and knead to soft dough.
Grease the worktop with some ghee. Heat a non-stick tawa.
Divide the dough into equal portions. Roll each portion into a thick disc.
Place one parantha at a time on it and cook on medium heat for 2-3 minutes.
Flip, apply some ghee and cook for 2-3 minutes more. Flip again, apply some ghee and cook till both sides are evenly golden.
Arrange them on a serving plate and serve hot.

title: Red Velvet Cupcakes
description: Red velvet cupcakes are a favourite of most children. Its soft velvety texture and bright red colour is indeed very endearing which win the hearts of adults too.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: Take butter in a bowl, add castor sugar and beat with an electric beater till the mixture is light and fluffy.
Sift refined flour, baking soda and cocoa powder directly over the butter mixture. Add salt and mix lightly.
Add vinegar, buttermilk and cochineal colour and beat till all the ingredients are well incorporated.
Preheat the air fryer at 180Â°C for 3 minutes.
Fill the prepared batter into a piping bag and pipe into individual silicon moulds.
Place the silicon moulds in the air fryer basket, fit the basket to the air fryer and air fry for 12 minutes.
Bring the basket out and let the cupcakes cool down to room temperature.
To prepare the frosting, take butter in a bowl, add cream cheese and icing sugar and beat with an electric beater till light and fluffy.
Add whipped cream and vanilla essence and fold in with light hands.
Transfer this mixture into a piping bag fitted with a star nozzle and pipe on cooled cupcakes.
Place the cupcakes on a serving platter and serve.

title: Saffron Pumpkin Khichadi
description: Saffron elevates this khichadi to another level with its flavour. Pumpkin adds its nutrients and a lovely golden colour. A comfort food at its best.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
main_ingredients: Split skinless green gram (dhuli moong dal)

ingredients.raw: A generous pinch of saffron + for garnish
ingredients.name: A generous pinch of saffron + for garnish

ingredients.raw: 150 grams red pumpkin, peeled and sliced
ingredients.name: red pumpkin, peeled and sliced

ingredients.raw: 100 grams pumpkin, peeled and cut into small pieces
ingredients.name: pumpkin, peeled and cut into small pieces

ingredients.raw: 1 cup short grain rice, soaked for 30 minutes and drained
ingredients.name: short grain rice, soaked for 30 minutes and drained

ingredients.raw: Â½ cup split skinless green gram (dhuli moong dal), soaked for 30 minutes and drained
ingredients.name: Â½ cup split skinless green gram , soaked for 30 minutes and drained

steps: Put rice in a pressure cooker, add split green gram, red pumpkin, salt, turmeric powder and 4 cups water. Close the lid and cook under pressure on medium heat till the pressure is released 3-4 times.
Open the lid once the pressure settles completely and mix well.
For tempering, heat ghee in a deep pan, add cumin seeds and once they start to change colour, add asafoetida, green chillies and curry leaves, mix and sautÃ© for 30 seconds.
Add garlic, mix and sautÃ© till golden brown. Add onion, mix and sautÃ© till golden brown.
Add pumpkin, mix and sautÃ© for 4-5 minutes.
Add salt and mix well. Reserve some tempering in a bowl. Add 1Â½ cups water and saffron and mix well.
Add the cooked mixture and mix well. Cook for 2-3 minutes or till the mixture is heated thoroughly.
Transfer into a serving plate, pour the reserved tempering on top. Garnish with saffron and coriander sprig.Drizzle ghee and serve hot.
tags: Split skinless green gram (dhuli moong dal)

title: Samo Pongal
description: Ven Pongal is a popular savoury comfort food, which is a creamy, porridge-like mixture of rice and yellow moong lentils seasoned with ghee, ginger, black pepper and curry leaves. Here we bring you version in which barnyard millet or samo replaces rice.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
main_ingredients: Spolit skinless green gram (dhuli moong dal)

ingredients.raw: 1 cup barnyard millet (samo), washed and drained
ingredients.name: barnyard millet , washed and drained

ingredients.raw: Â½ cup split skinless green gram (dhuli moong dal), soaked for 20 minutes and drained
ingredients.name: Â½ cup split skinless green gram , soaked for 20 minutes and drained

steps: Heat a pressure cooker, add barnyard millet, split skinless green gram, 4 cups water and salt and mix well.
Cover with the lid and cook under pressure on medium heat till the pressure is released 4 times. Open the lid once the pressure settles completely. Mash lightly.
Heat ghee in a non-stick kadai, add cashewnuts and sautÃ© till golden brown. Drain and set aside on a plate.
Add cumin seeds to the same kadai and once they start to change colour, add black peppercorns, ginger, curry leaves and asafoetida, mix and sautÃ© for 1 minute.
Add barnyard millet mixture and 1Â½ cups hot water, mix well and cook for 4-5 minutes. Add half the fried cashewnuts and mix well.
Transfer into a serving bowl, sprinkle remaining fried cashewnuts on top, garnish with coriander sprig and serve hot.
tags: Split skinless green gram (dhuli moong dal)

title: Samosa Chaat
description: This is for samosa lovers. Enjoy them in a different way. Use them to make a tangy chaat that is so delicious and also wholesome. Ideal to have with cups of hot tea especially on a monsoon evening.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: Date and tamarind chutney to drizzle
ingredients.name: Date and tamarind chutney to drizzle

steps: For each portion crush 2 samosas and arrange in a serving plate.
Drizzle yogurt, date and tamarind chutney and green chutney on top.
Sprinkle chaat masala, red chilli powder and black salt on top.
Sprinkle nylon sev, pomegranate pearls and masala peanuts.
Garnish with coriander sprigs and serve immediately.

title: Sandwich Idada
description: An iconic snack from Surat is a perfect choice for your Holi celebrations. This soft, spongy and subtly flavoured dhokla which will bring a smile with every bite and will become a favourite with whoever eats it.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
main_ingredients: Split skinless black gram (dhuli urad dal)

ingredients.raw: 1Â½ cups short grain rice, soaked for 1 hour and drained
ingredients.name: short grain rice, soaked for 1 hour and drained

ingredients.raw: Â½ cup split skinless black gram (dhuli urad dal), soaked for 1 hour and drained
ingredients.name: Â½ cup split skinless black gram , soaked for 1 hour and drained

ingredients.raw: Â½ cup thick pressed rice (jada poha), soaked for 10 minutes and drained
ingredients.name: Â½ cup thick pressed rice , soaked for 10 minutes and drained

ingredients.raw: 1 teaspoon fenugreek seeds (methi dana), soaked for 1 hour and drained
ingredients.name: fenugreek seeds , soaked for 1 hour and drained

ingredients.raw: Tamatar moongfalli ki chutney as required
ingredients.name: Tamatar moongfalli ki chutney as required

ingredients.raw: Chopped fresh coriander leaves for garnish
ingredients.name: Chopped fresh coriander leaves for garnish

steps: Put rice in a grinder jar, add split black gram, pressed rice, fenugreek seeds, yogurt and asafoetida and grind coarsely.
Add Â¾ cup water and continue to grind to a fine batter. Transfer into a bowl, cover and set aside to ferment for 8-10 hours.
Add ginger-green chilli paste, salt and sugar and mix well. Add fruit salt and mix well.
Heat sufficient water in a steamer. Grease dhokla plates with oil and pour a portion of batter in each plate.
Arrange the plates in the steamer, sprinkle crushed black peppercorns and red chilli powder on top and steam for 6-8 minutes.
Demould, apply some oil on top and allow to cool.
Place the plain side of 1 disc facing upwards and spread tamatar moongfalli ki chutney evenly. Place another disc on top with the plain side facing down and press gently. Cut into squares.
Arrange the squares in a serving plate, garnish with coriander leaves and serve.

title: Singhade Ka Halwa
description: Soft, sweet and perfect fasting dessert, this singhade ka halwa is not only easy to make but terrific to taste and quite healthy too. You neednâ€™t wait for vrat to enjoy it. Make it any day you want to and enjoy it with your family.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: Heat ghee in a non-stick pan, add water chestnut flour, mix and sautÃ© on medium heat for 10-12 minutes or till the mixture turns brown and fragrant.
Add sugar and 1Â½ cups hot water and mix till no lumps remain. Â Cook for 4-5 minutes.
Add milk, mix well and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Add green cardamom powder and mix well.
Add cashewnuts, pistachios and almonds and mix well
Transfer into a serving bowl, sprinkle cashewnuts, pistachios and almonds and serve immediately.

title: Spiced Hot Chocolate
description:
Warm cups, festive nights and a little indulgence in the guise of hot chocolate. And you go slurp slurp slurp.
This is Sanjeev Kapoor exclusive recipe.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: 4 pinches of cinnamon powder + to sprinkle
ingredients.name: pinches of cinnamon powder + to sprinkle

steps: For each portion, take 2-3 tablespoons chocolate chips in a glass mug. Add Â¾ cup hot milk and 1 pinch of cinnamon powder and stir till chocolate melts.
Top up with a few marshmallows, sprinkle some more cinnamon powder on top and serve hot.

title: Spicy Momo Soup
description: The popular stuffed dumplings from Nepal can be eaten with a dipping sauce or turned into a soup that is both delicious and comforting at the same time. Just make this recipe and you will want to make it often.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: Chop spring onion bulbs and greens separately and slice fresh red chilli.
Heat oil in a non-stick wok, add garlic and sautÃ© till light golden brown.
Add spring onion bulbs, fresh red chilli and salt and sautÃ© for 1 minute.
Add chicken stock and mix well. Once it comes to a boil, add Precia Chicken Momos and cook for 6-8 minutes.
Transfer into a serving bowl, sprinkle spring onion greens, drizzle chilli oil and serve hot.

title: Spring Rolls
description: A savoury Asian appetizer, it has vegetarian or non-vegetarian mixtures wrapped in thin spring roll sheets and deep-fried.  But here is a recipe that is ideal for weight watchers or those who have to avoid oily snacks because it is air fried.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: Â¼ cup chopped spring onion bulbs
ingredients.name: Â¼ cup chopped spring onion bulbs

ingredients.raw: 150 grams cabbage, cut into thin strips
ingredients.name: cabbage, cut into thin strips

ingredients.raw: 1 small carrot, peeled and cut into thin strips
ingredients.name: small carrot, peeled and cut into thin strips

ingredients.raw: 1 small green capsicum, seeded and cut into thin strips
ingredients.name: small green capsicum, seeded and cut into thin strips

ingredients.raw: 1 tablespoon sweet chilli sauce + for serving
ingredients.name: sweet chilli sauce + for serving

ingredients.raw: Â½ cup chopped spring onion greens
ingredients.name: Â½ cup chopped spring onion greens

steps: Heat oil in a non-stick pan, add garlic, ginger and green chilli, mix and sautÃ© for 1 minute.
Add spring onion bulbs, mix and sautÃ© till translucent. Add cabbage, carrot and green capsicum, mix and sautÃ© for 2-3 minutes.

Add salt, sweet chilli sauce, red chilli sauce and dark soy sauce, mix and saute for 1 minute. Add vinegar and spring onion greens and mix well. Take the pan off the heat and let the mixture cool down to room temperature.
Take refined flour in a bowl, add 3-4 tablespoons water and mix well to make thick slurry.
Halve each spring roll sheet, apply slurry on edges of each half, place the mixture in centre, fold in the vertical edges and roll tightly.
Preheat the air fryer at 190Â°C for 5 minutes. Spray some oil on the basket.
Arrange the spring rolls in the basket, fit it to the air fryer, spray some more oil on top and air fry for 12 minutes.
Bring the basket out, arrange the spring rolls on a serving platter and serve hot with sweet chilli sauce.

title: Tadgola Mango Drink
description:
Tired of drinking plain old water? Hereâ€™s a fun and flavourful way to stay hydrated! Whatâ€™s more it is nutritious too. 
This is Sanjeev Kapoor exclusive recipe.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: Chop ice apples.
Put some ice cubes in each of 4 serving glasses. Add 4-6 tablespoons mango pieces and 4-6 ice apples in each glass.
Add 4-6 tablespoons mango puree and 6-8 mint leaves in each glass. Top up with 1 cup coconut water in each glass and stir lightly.
Garnish each glass with a mango slice and serve chilled.

title: Tandoori Aloo
description: Here we will show you how to transform simple baby potatoes into a bold, flavour-packed snack using an air fryer. Coated in a creamy marinade and cooked till lightly crisp on outside and soft on the inside, this dish is perfect for parties or tea-time.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: Â¼ teaspoon dried fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi) powder
ingredients.name: Â¼ teaspoon dried fenugreek leaves  powder

steps: Dock the potatoes with a toothpick.
Boil some water in a deep non-stick pan, add Â½ teaspoon salt, potatoes and cook till half done. Drain and pat dry.

Take yogurt in a bowl, add turmeric powder, dried fenugreek leaves powder, dried mango powder, roasted gram flour, garam masala powder, red chilli powder, Â½ teaspoon salt, Kashmiri red chilli paste, ginger-garlic paste and mustard oil and mix well.
Add boiled potatoes and mix well. Set aside to marinate for 30-40 minutes.
Preheat the air fryer to 190Â° C for 2 minutes. Line air fryer basket with aluminium foil and arrange the potatoes in single layer. Fir the basket to the air fryer and air fry for 10 minutes.
Spray some oil and air fry for 5 minutes more.
Bring the basket out of the air fryer, arrange the potatoes in a bowl and serve hot with green chutney, lemon rings and lemon wedges.

title: Tandoori Ananas Chaat
description: Pineapple can be had in various ways â€“ just by itself or turned into a variety of dishes both sweet and savoury. Here we bring you a chaat where the pineapple is cooked in an air fryer, then mixed with chaat ingredients wherein each bite is unforgettable.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: Preheat the air fryer to 200Â° C.
Peel, core and quarter pineapple and keep them on a plate. Add salt, red chilli powder, chaat masala, black salt and castor sugar. Drizzle oil and mix well.
Arrange the pineapple pieces in the air fry basket, fix the basket to the air fryer and air fry for 15 minutes.
Bring the basket out, cut the pineapple quarters into 1-inch pieces and transfer into a bowl. Add English cucumber and tomato.
Drizzle green chutney, date and tamarind chutney and sweet chilli sauce.
Sprinkle chaat masala, black salt, masala chana dal and coriander leaves and mix well.
Transfer into individual serving bowls and serve.

title: Tandoori Chicken
description: Origins of tandoori chicken can be traced to Punjab but now it is popular not just in India but outside it too. Chicken on the bone is first marinated in spiced yogurt and then cooked in a clay tandoor. It can also be cooked in an oven or in an air fryer without really compromising the taste.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: 800 grams chicken on the bone, vertically halved
ingredients.name: chicken on the bone, vertically halved

ingredients.raw: Â½ teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder
ingredients.name: Â½ teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder

ingredients.raw: Â¼ teaspoon dried fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi) powder
ingredients.name: Â¼ teaspoon dried fenugreek leaves  powder

steps: Score the chicken pieces and transfer into a bowl. Add salt, Kashmiri red chilli powder and lemon juice and rub well over the chicken pieces. Set aside to marinate for 30 minutes.

For the second marinade, take yogurt in a bowl, add ginger-garlic paste, Kashmiri red chilli powder, salt, garam masala powder, dried fenugreek leaves powder, chaat masala, lemon juice and roasted gram flour and mix well. Add this to the chicken and mix well ensuring it covers the entire chicken. Set aside to marinate for 1-2 hours, preferably in the refrigerator.
Preheat the air fryer at 200Â°C for 3 minutes.
Cover the air fryer basket with aluminium foil and arrange the chicken pieces over it. Fit the basket to the air fryer and air fry for 22 minutes.
Bring the basket out, brush some melted butter on top and transfer the chicken onto a serving plate. Serve hot with green chutney, onion rings and lemon wedges.

title: Tandoori Chicken Popcorn
description: Crispy, juicy and flavour packed, these chicken popcorns are a delight to have at any party. Serve them as a snack with cups of hot tea or coffee or offer as starter at your party with cocktails or mocktails. They will definitely be much appreciated.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: Â½ teaspoon dried fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi) powder
ingredients.name: Â½ teaspoon dried fenugreek leaves  powder

steps: Cut chicken breast into small pieces.
Take yogurt in a bowl, add ginger-garlic paste, 1 teaspoon red chilli powder, tandoori masala, dried fenugreek leaves powder and salt.
Squeeze lemon and add mustard oil and mix well.Â Add chicken to this mixture and mix well. Set aside to marinate for 10-15 minutes.
Heat sufficient oil in a kadai.
Take refined flour in a bowl, add baking powder, Â½ teaspoon red chilli powder and salt and mix well.
Roll the chicken pieces in the refined flour mixture and arrange on a strainer. Dip in chilled water for 30 seconds.
Roll again in refined flour mixture till all the chicken pieces are completely coated.
Slide the chicken pieces into hot oil and deep-fry till golden brown and crisp. Drain on absorbent paper.
Transfer them into a serving bowl and serve hot with mint mayo and lemon wedges.

title: Tandoori Pomfret
description: Another tandoori speciality â€“ this time it is pomfret. Pomfrets are marinated in a spiced yogurt and cooked in an air fryer. Traditionally it is cooked in a clay tandoor but in the absence of a tandoor it can be cooked in an oven or in an air fryer.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: Â¼ teaspoon dried fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi) powder
ingredients.name: Â¼ teaspoon dried fenugreek leaves  powder

steps: Make incisions on pomfrets and transfer into a bowl. Add salt, lemon juice and Â½ teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder and mix well. Set aside for 20 minutes.

Take ginger-garlic paste in a bowl, add yogurt, carom seeds, roasted gram flour, dried fenugreek leaves powder, garam masala powder, Â½ teaspoon Kashmiri red chilli powder and mustard oil and whisk well.Apply this marinade all over the fish and set aside to marinate for 1 hour.
Preheat the air fryer at 180Â° C for 3 minutes.

Cover the air fryer basket with aluminium foil and arrange the pomfrets in it. Fit the basket into the air fryer and air fry for 10 minutes. Bring the basket out, flip the pomfrets, apply some butter on them. Fit the basket into the air fryer and air fry for 3 minutes more.
Bring the basket out again, transfer the pomfrets onto a serving plate and serve hot with green chutney, onion rings and mint sprigs.

title: Tendli Curry
description: Tendli or tindora is a vegetable which is used in various dishes in Karnataka. Here we bring you a tangy and flavourful curry that can be enjoyed with steamed rice or with chapattis.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: Smash ivy gourds lightly.
Heat a pan, add ivy gourds, 2 cups water, salt, turmeric powder and jaggery and mix well. Cook on medium heat for 10-12 minutes or till the ivy gourds are tender.
Meanwhile put coconut in a grinder jar, add tamarind, dried red chillies and Â½ cup water and grind to fine paste.
Add the ground paste to ivy gourds and cook on medium heat for 6-8 minutes.
Heat coconut oil in a pan, add mustard seeds and once they start to splutter, add remaining dried red chillies, garlic and curry leaves and sautÃ© till garlic turns golden brown.
Add this tempering to ivy gourd mixture and mix well.
Transfer into a serving bowl, garnish with fried curry leaves and serve hot with steamed rice.

title: Thukpa
description: The speciality of this dish from Tibet that it has the perfect balance of speed and flavour. A mixed vegetable soup poured over noodles is a food that will elevate your comfort to a next level.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: Thinly sliced spring onion greens for garnish
ingredients.name: Thinly sliced spring onion greens for garnish

steps: Put ginger, garlic, fresh red chillies, fresh coriander stems, tomato and onion in a grinder jar and grind to a fine paste.
Heat oil in a non-stick kadai. Add ground paste and cook on medium heat till oil separates.
Add vegetable stock and once the mixture comes to a boil, add cabbage, carrot, mushrooms and French beans and salt and cook for 5-6 minutes. Add vinegar and mix well.
Take noodles in a serving bowl, pour the soup over them, drizzle sesame oil, chilli oil, sprinkle spring onion greens and serve hot.

title: Veg Hakka Noodles
description: Vegetable Hakka Noodles is a popular Indo-Chinese dish that can be served with spicy gravy dish like vegetable Manchurian for best effect. Noodles and a variety of vegetables are stir-fried on high heat to get that much loved smoky flavour.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: Boil sufficient water in a deep pan, add 1 tablespoon oil, salt and noodles. Cook for 6-8 minutes. Drain the noodles and put them in a strainer, pour chilled water, drizzle some oil and mix well. Set aside.

Cut carrot into thin slices and further into strips. Shred cabbage thinly. Deseed and cut green capsicum into thin strips. Halve the onion and thinly slice it horizontally and separate the layers. Cut some spring onion greens diagonally into Â½ inch pieces and chop the remaining greens finely.
Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a non-stick wok, add garlic, ginger and green chillies, mix and sautÃ© till golden brown.
Add onion, carrot, cabbage and green capsicum, mix well and sautÃ© for 1 minute.
Add noodles, light soy sauce, white pepper powder, salt, stock cube powder and vinegar, toss well and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Add chopped spring onion greens and mix well.
Transfer into a serving bowl, sprinkle diagonally sliced spring onion greens and serve hot.

title: Veg Manchurian
description: Manchurian is a Indo-Chinese dish wherein fried chicken or cauliflower or dumplings of finely chopped vegetables are cooked in a spicy and flavourful sauce. It is now popular pan India and goes well with fried rice or hakka noodles.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: Deseed and finely chop green capsicum. Finely chop onion. Finely chop spring onion bulbs. Finely chop 1 spring onion green stalk and diagonally slice the other one.
Finely chop French beans. Finely grate cabbage and carrot into a bowl.
Heat sufficient oil in a kadai.
Put the grated cabbage and carrot in a muslin cloth, gather the edges and squeeze excess water. Transfer the squeezed mixture into another bowl.

Add chopped spring onion bulbs and greens. Add French beans, 2 tablespoons crushed ginger-garlic, 2 green chillies, light soy sauce, 1 teaspoon vinegar, salt, black pepper powder, 1 teaspoon red chilli sauce and 1 teaspoon green chilli sauce and mix well.
Add cornflour and refined flour and mix well. Divide into equal portions and shape each into a ball.
Slide the balls, a few at a time, into hot oil and deep-fry till golden brown and crisp. Drain on absorbent paper and set aside.
Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a non-stick wok, add 2 tablespoons crushed ginger-garlic, 1 chopped green chilli and onion, mix and sautÃ© till translucent.
Add green capsicum, mix and saute for 1 minute. Add dark soy sauce, 1 teaspoon green chilli sauce, 1 teaspoon red chilli sauce, salt, black pepper powder and mix well.
Add vegetable stock and mix well. Once the mixture comes to a boil, add cornflour slurry and mix well.
Add fried balls and toss well so that all the balls are covered with the sauce. Add 1 teaspoon vinegar and mix.
Sprinkle some diagonally sliced spring onion greens and mix well.
Transfer into a serving plate, garnish with remaining spring onion greens and serve hot.

title: Veg Pulao
description: This pulao is colourful with different coloured vegetables which also lend their individual flavour and nutrients. The aromatics add a wonderful aroma to this simple dish which can best be enjoyed with a any vegetable curry and raita.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: Wash basmati rice 2-3 times. Drain the water, add sufficient water and soak for 30 minutes.
Cut carrot and French beans into diamonds. Cut potatoes into 1 cm cubes.
Heat ghee in a pan, add cumin seeds and once they start change colour, add green cardamoms, black peppercorns, cloves, black cardamoms, bay leaf and cinnamon, mix and sautÃ© till fragrant.
Add onion, mix and sautÃ© till translucent. Add ginger-garlic paste, mix and sautÃ© for 1 minute. Add green chillies, mix and sautÃ© for 30 seconds.
Add carrot, potatoes and French beans and mix well. Add cauliflower and mix well. Add salt and mix.
Add 3 cups water, mix well and let the mixture come to a boil.
Drain rice and add to the pan. Add green peas and mix well. Cover and cook on low heat for 10-12 minutes.
Take the pan off the heat and set aside for 10 minutes without opening.
Transfer into a serving plate, garnish with coriander sprig and serve hot with vegetable curry and raita.

title: Vegetable Soup
description: If you want to enjoy the benefits of fresh vegetables then this soup is for you. It not only has a mild crunchiness of the vegetables but also is colourful and flavourful too.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: 1 small green capsicum, seeded and chopped
ingredients.name: small green capsicum, seeded and chopped

steps: Heat oil and butter in a deep non-stick pan, add ginger, garlic and green chilli, mix and sautÃ© till golden brown.
Add onion, mix and sautÃ© till translucent. Add cabbage, carrot, French beans, green peas, corn kernels, green capsicum and mushrooms, mix well and saute for 2-3 minutes.
Add black pepper powder and salt and mix well. Add macaroni and vegetable stock and cook for 8-10 minutes or till macaroni is cooked.
Transfer into a serving bowl and serve hot with lavash.

title: Vratwale Aloo
description: Simple, flavourful and wholesome this aloo ki sabzi tastes great with rajgire ke paranthe. But why wait to make it for your next vrat. Make it any time and serve it with any paranthe.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor
steps: Heat ghee in a non-stick kadai, add cumin seeds and once they start to change colour, add green chillies, mix and sautÃ© for a few seconds.
Mash potatoes roughly and add to the kadai. Add rock salt and yogurt, mix well and cook for 4-5 minutes.Add coconut and mix. Add 1 cup water, mix well and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Add coriander leaves and mix well.
Transfer into a serving bowl, garnish with coriander sprig and serve hot.
To serve the vrat ki thali, take some cucumber raita in one bowl, place the rajgire ke paranthe in the centre, aloo ki sabzi in another bowl and singhade ka halwa in a third bowl.

title: Zafrani Murgh Shorba
description: A comforting soup at its best with the rich aroma of saffron. Simmering chicken with aromatic spices and saffron to make a golden-hued and high protein broth can be served at a special party too. Serve it with garlic toast for best effect.
author: Sanjeev Kapoor

ingredients.raw: A generous pinch of saffron (zafran/ kesar)
ingredients.name: A generous pinch of saffron

steps: Score chicken leg on both sides.
Heat 2 tablespoons ghee in a pressure cooker, add caraway seeds, black peppercorns, green cardamoms, cinnamon, black cardamom and bay leaf, mix and sautÃ© till fragrant.
Add garlic cloves, onion and fresh coriander stems and mix well. Add green chillies, mix and sautÃ© till translucent.
Add ginger-garlic paste, chicken bones and salt, mix and sautÃ© on high heat for 3-4 minutes.
Add turmeric powder and mix well.
Add chicken leg and mix well. Add 3-4 cups water, adjust salt and mix well.
Cover with cooker the lid and cook under pressure on medium heat till the pressure is released 2-3 times.
Open the lid once the pressure settles completely. Remove the leg and shred the meat.
Heat 2 tablespoons ghee in a pan, add whole wheat flour and sautÃ©, stirring continuously, on medium heat for 2-3 minutes.
Add saffron and mix well. Strain the stock directly into the pan, mix well and bring the mixture to a boil.
Add coconut milk, mix well cook for 3-4 minutes. Add shredded chicken and mix well.
Transfer into a serving bowl, garnish with coriander sprig and serve hot.
